docs.title: Colors
docs.text:
class Colors

This class defined a good set of ANSI colors.

Tip

It is recomended to use those through streams : std::cout << Flint::Colors::GREEN << "Hacker time" << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl;

Info

Please ALWAYS remember to close your colors with Flint::Colors::RESET. Failure to do si might result in broken displays, with weird colors everywhere

docs.title: CxxABI
docs.text:
class CxxABI

Wrapper around usefull C++/C low level functions

Got demangle, backtrace...

Tip

This class is only made of static functions. It does not contain constructor, nor destructor This means that you don't need to instanciate it, and only use it as if it was a namespace.

static std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> getBacktrace()

Retreive the execution trace

Return Vector of pair, string>. I'll explain

The idea is to retrieive the file, line, and function. So that's what we do : pair or : (file, line), function

class Exception: public std::exception

Wrapper arround default std::exception

Store the the exception description as std::string, and store some usefull localisation informations: Filename, Line, and Function Name. This is automated using the throw_exception preprocessor directive.

Tip

Take a look at the NotImplementedError to see how easy it is to implmeent your own Flint::Exception.

Display the error, given specific positional infos (like the constructor).

docs.title: what
docs.text:
const char* what() const noexcept override

Required override, deprecated by show and catch_exception.

Info

This is the required std::exception override. It'll return the _what as a const char*. While this will work, prefer using the show function or the catch_excpetion preprocessor directive :

Example

try {
throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::Exception, "Sample testing
exception"); } catch (const Flint::Exceptions::Exception& e) {
std::cerr << catch_exception(e) << std::endl;
}
Return The _what as a C string

docs.title: Inspection
docs.text:
template<typename T> class Inspection

Base class to implement simplet self-reflexion in C++

T Child class itself

Tip

When creating a child class, define as follow : 

class ChildClass: public Inspection<ChildClass>
This will extand the ChildClass with the self-reflexion class Inspection

Warning

If the class you want to extand as a reflected class will be a parent class (I.E. You will create childs of this "target" class), you might want to mark inheritence as virtual. This is needed if your child class also need to be reflected. This way, there is no confilict between the parent reflexion, and the child reflexion.

docs.title: Functions
docs.text: Name Description getClassName Return the name of the reflected class Return : The demangled name of the class, or just the name, if demangleing failed str Return a string representation of the class !!! tip This function is not intended to be used. This is because this functions display the reflection. You might prefer to override this function in yout child class to display informations that are more relevant to your specific class. !!! example You have a Macro available to make the str function really easilly : cpp display_attr(attribute) Which will automaticly translate to cpp Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << "attribute" << Flint::Colors::RESET << "=" << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute << Flint::Colors::RESET There is also a make_str macro, which you can use as follow : cpp return make_str("a=12" << ", " << "b=5"); that becomes cpp return ({std::stringstream ss; ss << "<" << this->getClassName() << " at " << std::hex << this << ": " << "a=12" << ", " << "b=5" << ">"; ss.str()}) It means you can define the str function of your class as like that : cpp // Provided you have 3 attributes called a, b and c return make_str(display_attr(a) << ", " << display_attr(b) << ", " << display_attr(c)); Return : The string representation of the class

docs.title: str
docs.text:
virtual std::string str() const

Return a string representation of the class

Tip

This function is not intended to be used. This is because this functions display the reflection. You might prefer to override this function in yout child class to display informations that are more relevant to your specific class.

Example

You have a Macro available to make the str function really easilly : 

display_attr(attribute)
Which will automaticly translate to 
Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << "attribute" << Flint::Colors::RESET <<
"=" << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute <<
Flint::Colors::RESET
There is also a make_str macro, which you can use as follow : 
return make_str("a=12" << ", " << "b=5");
that becomes 
return ({std::stringstream ss; ss << "<" << this->getClassName()
<< " at " << std::hex << this << ": " << "a=12" << ", " << "b=5"
<< ">"; ss.str()})
It means you can define the str function of your class as like that : 
// Provided you have 3 attributes called a, b and c
return make_str(display_attr(a) << ", " << display_attr(b) << ",
" << display_attr(c));

Return The string representation of the class

#include <iostream>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <string>

#include "tcp_connect/TCPConnectProtocol.hpp"
#include "tcp_bind/TCPBindProtocol.hpp"
#include "udp/UDPProtocol.hpp"
#include "UnixSessionManager.hpp"

int main()
{
PluginSystem::TCPConnectProtocol tcp("127.0.0.1", 5555);
PluginSystem::UDPProtocol udp("127.0.0.1", 6666);

tcp.toggle();   // active le plugin
try {
tcp.connect();
std::cout << "TCP connected? " << tcp.isConnected() << '\n';
tcp.send("hello from tcp\n");
std::cout << "TCP payload sent\n";
auto tcpData = tcp.receive(1024, true);
if (tcpData.empty())
std::cout << "TCP no data available (send something back to port 5555)\n";
else
std::cout << "TCP received: " << tcpData << '\n';
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cout << "TCP failure: " << e.what() << '\n';
}

tcp.toggle();   // le toggle off doit forcer la déconnexion
std::cout << "TCP connected after toggle? " << tcp.isConnected() << '\n';

try {
udp.connect(); // échoue car toggle pas encore appelé
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cout << "Expected failure: " << e.what() << '\n';
}

udp.toggle();
udp.connect();
std::cout << "UDP ready? " << udp.isConnected() << '\n';
try {
udp.send("hello from udp\n");
std::cout << "UDP payload sent\n";
auto udpData = udp.receive(1024, true);
if (udpData.empty())
std::cout << "UDP no data available (echo something to 127.0.0.1:6666)\n";
else
std::cout << "UDP received: " << udpData << '\n';
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cout << "UDP failure: " << e.what() << '\n';
}
udp.disconnect();
std::cout << "UDP ready after disconnect? " << udp.isConnected() << '\n';

if (const char *runBind = std::getenv("RUN_TCP_BIND_DEMO")) {
std::cout << "\n[Bind demo requested]\n";
PluginSystem::TCPBindProtocol tcpBind("127.0.0.1", 7777);
tcpBind.toggle();
try {
tcpBind.connect();
std::cout << "TCP bind listening on 127.0.0.1:7777 (connect via: nc 127.0.0.1 7777)\n";
auto incoming = tcpBind.receive(1024, false);
std::cout << "TCP bind received: " << incoming << '\n';
tcpBind.send("echo> " + incoming);
std::cout << "TCP bind echoed data\n";
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cout << "TCP bind failure: " << e.what() << '\n';
}
tcpBind.disconnect();
}

if (const char *runUnix = std::getenv("RUN_UNIX_SOCKET_DEMO")) {
std::cout << "\n[Unix session manager demo]\n";
PluginSystem::UnixSessionManager manager("unix_demo");
try {
auto socketPath = manager.createSession("demo");
manager.setActiveSession("demo");
std::cout << "Unix session waiting on: " << socketPath << '\n';

// Non bloquant pour éviter de geler la démo CLI.
auto incoming = manager.readActive(1024, false);
if (incoming.empty())
std::cout << "No local payload yet (try: nc -U -N " << socketPath << ")\n";
else {
std::cout << "Unix session received: " << incoming << '\n';
manager.writeActive("ack> " + incoming);
std::cout << "Unix session echoed data\n";
}
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cout << "Unix session failure: " << e.what() << '\n';
}
}

return 0;
}

#include "TCPBindProtocol.hpp"

#include <cerrno>
#include <cstring>
#include <netdb.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <unistd.h>

#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace PluginSystem
{
TCPBindProtocol::TCPBindProtocol(const nlohmann::json &config)
: AProtocol(config)
, _host(config.value("host", "0.0.0.0"))
, _port(config.value("port", static_cast<std::uint16_t>(0)))
, _backlog(config.value("backlog", 1))
, _listenFd(-1)
, _clientFd(-1)
, _active(false)
, _connected(false)
{
if (_host.empty())
throw std::invalid_argument("TCPBindProtocol requires a non-empty host");
if (_port == 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("TCPBindProtocol requires a non-zero port");
if (_backlog <= 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("TCPBindProtocol backlog must be > 0");
}

TCPBindProtocol::~TCPBindProtocol()
{
try {
disconnect();
} catch (...) {
}
}

void TCPBindProtocol::toggle()
{
_active = !_active;
if (!_active)
disconnect();
}

void TCPBindProtocol::connect()
{
if (!_active)
throw std::runtime_error("Protocol TCPBindProtocol is disabled");
if (_connected)
return;

if (_listenFd == -1)
openListener();

_connected = true;
}

void TCPBindProtocol::disconnect()
{
if (!_connected && _listenFd == -1)
return;

closeSockets();
_connected = false;
}

void TCPBindProtocol::send(const std::string &data)
{
if (_clientFd == -1)
acceptClient();

const char *buffer = data.data();
std::size_t total  = 0;
const std::size_t size = data.size();

while (total < size) {
ssize_t sent = ::send(_clientFd, buffer + total, size - total, 0);
if (sent == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
throw std::runtime_error(
"TCPBindProtocol send failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}
total += static_cast<std::size_t>(sent);
}
}

std::string TCPBindProtocol::receive(std::size_t maxBytes, bool nonBlocking)
{
if (_clientFd == -1)
acceptClient();
if (maxBytes == 0)
return {};

std::string buffer(maxBytes, '\0');
const int flags = nonBlocking ? MSG_DONTWAIT : 0;

ssize_t received = ::recv(_clientFd, buffer.data(), buffer.size(), flags);
if (received == -1) {
if (nonBlocking && (errno == EWOULDBLOCK || errno == EAGAIN))
return {};
throw std::runtime_error(
"TCPBindProtocol recv failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}
if (received == 0) {
::close(_clientFd);
_clientFd = -1;
throw std::runtime_error("TCPBindProtocol peer disconnected");
}

buffer.resize(static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
return buffer;
}

void TCPBindProtocol::openListener()
{
addrinfo hints{};
hints.ai_family   = AF_UNSPEC;
hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
hints.ai_flags    = AI_PASSIVE;

addrinfo *result = nullptr;
const std::string portString = std::to_string(_port);
int ret = ::getaddrinfo(_host.c_str(), portString.c_str(), &hints, &result);
if (ret != 0)
throw std::runtime_error("TCPBindProtocol getaddrinfo failed: "
+ std::string(::gai_strerror(ret)));

int fd = -1;
for (addrinfo *rp = result; rp != nullptr; rp = rp->ai_next) {
fd = ::socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
if (fd == -1)
continue;

int opt = 1;
::setsockopt(fd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_REUSEADDR, &opt, sizeof(opt));

if (::bind(fd, rp->ai_addr, rp->ai_addrlen) == 0) {
if (::listen(fd, _backlog) == 0)
break;
}

::close(fd);
fd = -1;
}

::freeaddrinfo(result);

if (fd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error("TCPBindProtocol failed to bind/listen on "
+ _host + ":" + std::to_string(_port));

_listenFd = fd;
}

void TCPBindProtocol::closeSockets()
{
if (_clientFd != -1) {
::close(_clientFd);
_clientFd = -1;
}
if (_listenFd != -1) {
::close(_listenFd);
_listenFd = -1;
}
}

void TCPBindProtocol::acceptClient()
{
if (_listenFd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error("TCPBindProtocol listener is not open");

sockaddr_storage addr{};
socklen_t        len = sizeof(addr);
int              fd  = ::accept(_listenFd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), &len);
if (fd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error(
"TCPBindProtocol accept failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));

if (_clientFd != -1)
::close(_clientFd);
_clientFd = fd;
}
}

#ifdef PROTOCOL_PLUGIN_EXPORT
extern "C" PluginSystem::AProtocol *create_protocol(const char *configJson)
{
nlohmann::json config = nlohmann::json::object();
if (configJson && *configJson)
config = nlohmann::json::parse(configJson);
return new PluginSystem::TCPBindProtocol(config);
}

extern "C" void destroy_protocol(PluginSystem::AProtocol *protocol)
{
delete protocol;
}
#endif

void TCPConnectProtocol::send(const std::string &data)

{

return {};

std::string buffer(maxBytes, '\0');
const int flags = nonBlocking ? MSG_DONTWAIT : 0;

ssize_t received = ::recv(_socketFd, buffer.data(), buffer.size(), flags);
if (received == -1) {
if (nonBlocking && (errno == EWOULDBLOCK || errno == EAGAIN))
return {};
throw std::runtime_error(
"TCPConnectProtocol recv failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}
if (received == 0) {
closeSocket();

}

buffer.resize(static_cast<std::size_t>(received));

}

void TCPConnectProtocol::openSocket()
{
addrinfo hints{};

int fd = -1;
for (addrinfo *rp = result; rp != nullptr; rp = rp->ai_next) {

if (fd == -1)
continue;
if (::connect(fd, rp->ai_addr, rp->ai_addrlen) == 0)

::close(fd);

}

::freeaddrinfo(result);

throw std::runtime_error("TCPConnectProtocol failed to connect to " + _host + ":"

}

void TCPConnectProtocol::closeSocket()
{
if (_socketFd == -1)
return;
::close(_socketFd);

throw std::runtime_error("Protocol UDPProtocol is disabled");
if (_connected)
return;
openSocket();
_connected = true;
}

void UDPProtocol::disconnect()
{
if (!_connected)
return;
closeSocket();
_connected = false;
}

void UDPProtocol::send(const std::string &data)
{
if (_socketFd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error("UDPProtocol is not connected");
if (_remoteAddrLen == 0)
throw std::runtime_error("UDPProtocol has no remote address configured");

ssize_t sent = ::sendto(_socketFd, data.data(), data.size(), 0,
reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&_remoteAddr), _remoteAddrLen);

if (sent == -1)
throw std::runtime_error(
"UDPProtocol send failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}

std::string UDPProtocol::receive(std::size_t maxBytes, bool nonBlocking)
{
if (_socketFd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error("UDPProtocol is not connected");
if (maxBytes == 0)
return {};

std::string buffer(maxBytes, '\0');
const int flags = nonBlocking ? MSG_DONTWAIT : 0;

ssize_t received = ::recvfrom(
_socketFd,
buffer.data(),
buffer.size(),
flags,
nullptr,
nullptr);

if (received == -1) {
if (nonBlocking && (errno == EWOULDBLOCK || errno == EAGAIN))
return {};
throw std::runtime_error(
"UDPProtocol recv failed: " + std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}

buffer.resize(static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
return buffer;
}

void UDPProtocol::openSocket()
{
addrinfo hints{};
hints.ai_family   = AF_UNSPEC;
hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_DGRAM;

addrinfo *result = nullptr;
const std::string portString = std::to_string(_port);
int ret = ::getaddrinfo(_host.c_str(), portString.c_str(), &hints, &result);
if (ret != 0)
throw std::runtime_error("UDPProtocol getaddrinfo failed: "
+ std::string(::gai_strerror(ret)));

int fd = -1;
for (addrinfo *rp = result; rp != nullptr; rp = rp->ai_next) {
fd = ::socket(rp->ai_family, rp->ai_socktype, rp->ai_protocol);
if (fd == -1)
continue;
_remoteAddrLen = static_cast<socklen_t>(rp->ai_addrlen);
std::memcpy(&_remoteAddr, rp->ai_addr, _remoteAddrLen);
break;
}

::freeaddrinfo(result);

if (fd == -1 || _remoteAddrLen == 0)
throw std::runtime_error("UDPProtocol failed to prepare remote address "
+ _host + ":" + std::to_string(_port));

_socketFd = fd;
}

void UDPProtocol::closeSocket()
{
if (_socketFd == -1)
return;
::close(_socketFd);
_socketFd      = -1;
_remoteAddrLen = 0;
}
}

/*

** tek4

#include "tool-agentinfo.hpp"

#include <chrono>
#include <cerrno>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <cstring>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <locale>
#include <poll.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <fcntl.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <sys/un.h>
#include <unistd.h>

namespace {
constexpr std::size_t MAX_LINE_SIZE = 4096;
constexpr const char *AGENTINFO_MARKER = "AB_AGENTINFO";

struct AgentinfoInput {
std::string socketPath;
std::string agentId;
int timeoutMs = 1500;
};

struct AgentinfoPayload {
std::string ip;
std::string os;
std::string hostname;
std::string arch;
};

struct AgentinfoResult {
bool ok = false;
std::string error;
AgentinfoPayload payload;
};

enum class PollStatus {
READY,
TIMEOUT,
ERROR
};

std::string jsonEscape(const std::string &value)
{
std::string out;
out.reserve(value.size());
for (unsigned char c : value) {
switch (c) {
case '\\': out += "\\\\"; break;
case '"': out += "\\\""; break;
case '\n': out += "\\n"; break;
case '\r': out += "\\r"; break;
case '\t': out += "\\t"; break;
default:
if (c < 0x20) {
char buf[7];
std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "\\u%04x", c);
out += buf;
} else {
out += static_cast<char>(c);
}
}
}
return out;
}

std::string trim(const std::string &value)
{
const std::size_t first = value.find_first_not_of(" \t\r\n");
if (first == std::string::npos)
return "";
const std::size_t last = value.find_last_not_of(" \t\r\n");
return value.substr(first, last - first + 1);
}

bool parseInput(const std::string &input, AgentinfoInput &out)
{
out.socketPath = trim(input);
return !out.socketPath.empty();
}

PollStatus pollReady(int fd, short events, int timeoutMs)
{
pollfd pfd{};
pfd.fd = fd;
pfd.events = events;

int rc = 0;
do {
rc = ::poll(&pfd, 1, timeoutMs);
} while (rc == -1 && errno == EINTR);

if (rc == 0)
return PollStatus::TIMEOUT;
if (rc < 0)
return PollStatus::ERROR;
if (pfd.revents & events)
return PollStatus::READY;
return PollStatus::ERROR;
}

bool parseReply(const std::string &line, AgentinfoPayload &payload)
{
std::istringstream stream(line);
std::vector<std::string> tokens;
std::string token;
while (stream >> token)
tokens.push_back(token);

if (tokens.size() != 5)
return false;
if (tokens[0] != AGENTINFO_MARKER)
return false;

payload.ip = tokens[1];
payload.os = tokens[2];
payload.hostname = tokens[3];
payload.arch = tokens[4];

return !payload.ip.empty() && !payload.os.empty() && !payload.hostname.empty() && !payload.arch.empty();
}

AgentinfoResult requestAgentinfo(const AgentinfoInput &input)
{
AgentinfoResult result;

sockaddr_un addr{};
if (input.socketPath.size() >= sizeof(addr.sun_path)) {
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

int fd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (fd == -1) {
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

int flags = ::fcntl(fd, F_GETFL, 0);
if (flags == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

addr.sun_family = AF_UNIX;
std::snprintf(addr.sun_path, sizeof(addr.sun_path), "%s", input.socketPath.c_str());

int rc = ::connect(fd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), sizeof(addr));
if (rc == -1 && errno != EINPROGRESS) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

if (rc == -1) {
const PollStatus status = pollReady(fd, POLLOUT, input.timeoutMs);
if (status == PollStatus::TIMEOUT) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "timeout";
return result;
}
if (status == PollStatus::ERROR) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

int soError = 0;
socklen_t soLen = sizeof(soError);
if (::getsockopt(fd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, &soError, &soLen) == -1 || soError != 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

const std::string request =
"IP=$(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}'); "
"OS=$(uname -s); "
"HOSTNAME=$(hostname -s); "
"ARCH=$(uname -m); "
"echo "
+ std::string(AGENTINFO_MARKER)
+ " $IP $OS $HOSTNAME $ARCH\n";
std::size_t total = 0;
while (total < request.size()) {
ssize_t sent = ::send(fd, request.data() + total, request.size() - total,
#ifdef MSG_NOSIGNAL
MSG_NOSIGNAL
#else
0
#endif
);
if (sent == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}
total += static_cast<std::size_t>(sent);
}

const PollStatus readStatus = pollReady(fd, POLLIN, input.timeoutMs);
if (readStatus == PollStatus::TIMEOUT) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "timeout";
return result;
}
if (readStatus == PollStatus::ERROR) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}

std::string responseBuffer;
responseBuffer.reserve(256);

const auto deadline = std::chrono::steady_clock::now()
+ std::chrono::milliseconds(input.timeoutMs);

while (std::chrono::steady_clock::now() < deadline) {
const auto remaining = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(
deadline - std::chrono::steady_clock::now()).count();
const int waitMs = remaining > 0 ? static_cast<int>(remaining) : 0;

const PollStatus status = pollReady(fd, POLLIN, waitMs);
if (status == PollStatus::TIMEOUT) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "timeout";
return result;
}
if (status == PollStatus::ERROR) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}

char chunk[256];
const ssize_t received = ::recv(fd, chunk, sizeof(chunk), 0);
if (received == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}
if (received == 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}

responseBuffer.append(chunk, static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
if (responseBuffer.size() > MAX_LINE_SIZE) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "bad_reply";
return result;
}

std::size_t newlinePos = std::string::npos;
while ((newlinePos = responseBuffer.find('\n')) != std::string::npos) {
std::string line = responseBuffer.substr(0, newlinePos);
responseBuffer.erase(0, newlinePos + 1);

AgentinfoPayload payload;
if (parseReply(line, payload)) {
::close(fd);
result.ok = true;
result.payload = payload;
return result;
}
}
}

::close(fd);
result.error = "bad_reply";
return result;
}

std::string buildOutput(const std::string &agentId, const AgentinfoResult &result)
{
std::ostringstream out;
out.imbue(std::locale::classic());

out << "{"
<< "\"type\":\"agentinfo\"," << "\"agent_id\":\"" << jsonEscape(agentId) << "\","
<< "\"ok\":" << (result.ok ? "true" : "false") << ","
<< "\"ip\":";

if (result.ok)
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.payload.ip) << "\"";
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"os\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.payload.os) << "\"";
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"hostname\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.payload.hostname) << "\"";
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"arch\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.payload.arch) << "\"";
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"error\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "null";
else
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.error.empty() ? "recv_failed" : result.error) << "\"";

out << "}";
return out.str();
}
}

void PluginSystem::ToolAgentinfo::toggle()
{
isActive = !isActive;
}

void PluginSystem::ToolAgentinfo::execute(const nlohmann::json &params)
{
AgentinfoInput input;
input.agentId = params.value("agent_id", std::string("shell"));
input.timeoutMs = params.value("timeout_ms", input.timeoutMs);

if (input.timeoutMs <= 0)
input.timeoutMs = 1500;

std::string inputLine;
if (!std::getline(std::cin, inputLine))
inputLine.clear();

if (!parseInput(inputLine, input)) {
AgentinfoResult result;
result.error = "parse_error";
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
return;
}

if (!isActive) {
AgentinfoResult result;
result.error = "disabled";
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
return;
}

const AgentinfoResult result = requestAgentinfo(input);
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
}

{
return new PluginSystem::ToolAgentinfo();
}

#include "tool-persistence-ssh.hpp"

#include <unistd.h>

namespace {

struct PersistenceInput {
std::string socketPath;
std::string agentId;
int timeoutMs = 2500;
std::vector<std::string> publicKeys;
};

struct PersistenceResult {
bool ok = false;
std::string error;
int appliedKeys = 0;
};

std::string trim(const std::string &value)
{
const std::size_t first = value.find_first_not_of(" \t\r\n");
if (first == std::string::npos)
return "";
const std::size_t last = value.find_last_not_of(" \t\r\n");
return value.substr(first, last - first + 1);
}

std::string jsonEscape(const std::string &value)
{
std::string out;
out.reserve(value.size());
for (unsigned char c : value) {
switch (c) {
case '\\': out += "\\\\"; break;
case '"': out += "\\\""; break;
case '\n': out += "\\n"; break;
case '\r': out += "\\r"; break;
case '\t': out += "\\t"; break;
default:
if (c < 0x20) {
char buf[7];
std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "\\u%04x", c);
out += buf;
} else {
out += static_cast<char>(c);
}
}
}
return out;
}

std::string shellSingleQuote(const std::string &value)
{
std::string out;
out.reserve(value.size() + 8);
out.push_back('\'');
for (char c : value) {
if (c == '\'') {
out += "'\"'\"'";
} else {
out.push_back(c);
}
}
out.push_back('\'');
return out;
}

bool isSafePublicKeyText(const std::string &value)
{
if (value.empty() || value.size() > 8192)
return false;
for (unsigned char c : value) {
if (c == '\n' || c == '\r' || c == '\0')
return false;
}
return true;
}

bool pollReady(int fd, short events, int timeoutMs, std::string &error)
{
pollfd pfd{};
pfd.fd = fd;
pfd.events = events;

int rc = 0;
do {
rc = ::poll(&pfd, 1, timeoutMs);
} while (rc == -1 && errno == EINTR);

if (rc == 0) {
error = "timeout";
return false;
}
if (rc < 0) {
error = "poll_failed";
return false;
}
if (pfd.revents & events)
return true;

error = "poll_failed";
return false;
}

bool parseInputLine(const std::string &line, PersistenceInput &out, std::string &error)
{
out.socketPath = trim(line);
if (out.socketPath.empty()) {
error = "invalid_input";
return false;
}
return true;
}

std::string buildPersistenceCommand(const std::vector<std::string> &publicKeys,
const std::string &doneToken)
{
std::string command =
"mkdir -p ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh && touch ~/.ssh/authorized_keys && chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys; ";

for (const auto &publicKey : publicKeys) {
const std::string escaped = shellSingleQuote(publicKey);
command += "if ! grep -Fqx -- " + escaped + " ~/.ssh/authorized_keys; then echo " + escaped + " >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys; fi; ";
}

command += "echo " + doneToken + "\\n";
return command;
}

PersistenceResult applyPersistence(const PersistenceInput &input)
{
PersistenceResult result;

if (input.publicKeys.empty()) {
result.error = "no_public_keys";
return result;
}

sockaddr_un addr{};
if (input.socketPath.size() >= sizeof(addr.sun_path)) {
result.error = "socket_path_too_long";
return result;
}

int fd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (fd == -1) {
result.error = "socket_create_failed";
return result;
}

int flags = ::fcntl(fd, F_GETFL, 0);
if (flags == -1 || ::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "socket_flags_failed";
return result;
}

addr.sun_family = AF_UNIX;
std::snprintf(addr.sun_path, sizeof(addr.sun_path), "%s", input.socketPath.c_str());

int rc = ::connect(fd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), sizeof(addr));
if (rc == -1 && errno != EINPROGRESS) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

if (rc == -1) {
std::string pollError;
if (!pollReady(fd, POLLOUT, input.timeoutMs, pollError)) {
::close(fd);
result.error = pollError == "timeout" ? "timeout" : "connect_failed";
return result;
}
int soError = 0;
socklen_t soLen = sizeof(soError);
if (::getsockopt(fd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, &soError, &soLen) == -1 || soError != 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "socket_flags_failed";
return result;
}

const std::string doneToken = "__AB_PERSIST_DONE__";
const std::string command = buildPersistenceCommand(input.publicKeys, doneToken);

std::size_t total = 0;
while (total < command.size()) {
const ssize_t sent = ::send(fd, command.data() + total, command.size() - total,
#ifdef MSG_NOSIGNAL
MSG_NOSIGNAL
#else
0
#endif
);
if (sent < 0) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "send_failed";
return result;
}
total += static_cast<std::size_t>(sent);
}

std::string response;
response.reserve(1024);
char chunk[256];

while (true) {
std::string pollError;
if (!pollReady(fd, POLLIN, input.timeoutMs, pollError)) {
::close(fd);
result.error = pollError == "timeout" ? "timeout" : "recv_failed";
return result;
}

const ssize_t received = ::recv(fd, chunk, sizeof(chunk), 0);
if (received < 0) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}
if (received == 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connection_closed";
return result;
}

response.append(chunk, static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
if (response.find(doneToken) != std::string::npos) {
result.ok = true;
result.appliedKeys = static_cast<int>(input.publicKeys.size());
::close(fd);
return result;
}
if (response.size() > 32768) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "invalid_response";
return result;
}
}
}

std::string buildOutput(const PersistenceInput &input, const PersistenceResult &result)
{
std::ostringstream out;
out << "{"
<< "\"type\":\"persistence\"," << "\"agent_id\":\"" << jsonEscape(input.agentId) << "\"," << "\"ok\":" << (result.ok ? "true" : "false") << "," << "\"applied_keys\":" << (result.ok ? std::to_string(result.appliedKeys) : "null") << "," << "\"error\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "null";
else
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.error.empty() ? "unknown_error" : result.error) << "\"";
out << "}";
return out.str();
}

} // namespace

void PluginSystem::ToolPersistenceSsh::toggle()
{
isActive = !isActive;
}

void PluginSystem::ToolPersistenceSsh::execute(const nlohmann::json &params)
{
PersistenceInput input;
input.agentId = params.value("agent_id", std::string("shell"));
input.timeoutMs = params.value("timeout_ms", input.timeoutMs);
if (input.timeoutMs <= 0)
input.timeoutMs = 2500;

if (params.contains("public_keys") && params["public_keys"].is_array()) {
for (const auto &entry : params["public_keys"]) {
if (!entry.is_string())
continue;
const std::string key = trim(entry.get<std::string>());
if (isSafePublicKeyText(key)) {
input.publicKeys.push_back(key);
}
}
}

if (!isActive) {
PersistenceResult disabled;
disabled.ok = false;
disabled.error = "disabled";
std::cout << buildOutput(input, disabled) << std::endl;
return;
}

std::string inputLine;
if (!std::getline(std::cin, inputLine))
inputLine.clear();

std::string parseError;
if (!parseInputLine(inputLine, input, parseError)) {
PersistenceResult failed;

}

}

namespace {
constexpr const char *PING_MARKER = "__AB_PING_OK__";

void runMockServer(const std::string &socketPath,
std::atomic<bool> &ready)
{
std::filesystem::create_directories(std::filesystem::path(socketPath).parent_path());
::unlink(socketPath.c_str());

int listenFd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (listenFd == -1) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] Failed to create socket\n";
return;
}

sockaddr_un addr{};
addr.sun_family = AF_UNIX;
std::snprintf(addr.sun_path, sizeof(addr.sun_path), "%s", socketPath.c_str());

if (::bind(listenFd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), sizeof(addr)) == -1) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] bind failed: " << std::strerror(errno) << "\n";
::close(listenFd);
return;
}

if (::listen(listenFd, 1) == -1) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] listen failed: " << std::strerror(errno) << "\n";
::close(listenFd);
return;
}

ready.store(true);

int clientFd = ::accept(listenFd, nullptr, nullptr);
if (clientFd == -1) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] accept failed: " << std::strerror(errno) << "\n";
::close(listenFd);
return;
}

std::string buffer;
char chunk[128];
while (buffer.find('\n') == std::string::npos) {
ssize_t received = ::recv(clientFd, chunk, sizeof(chunk), 0);
if (received <= 0)
break;
buffer.append(chunk, static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
if (buffer.size() > 1024)
break;
}

if (buffer.find("echo __AB_PING_OK__") != std::string::npos) {
std::string reply = std::string(PING_MARKER) + "\n";
::send(clientFd, reply.data(), reply.size(), 0);
}

::close(clientFd);
::close(listenFd);
::unlink(socketPath.c_str());
}
}

int main(int argc, char **argv)
{
const std::string socketPath = "/tmp/listeners/unix_demo/session_demo.sock";
std::string pluginPath = "Plugins/tool-ping.so";
if (argc > 1)
pluginPath = argv[1];

std::atomic<bool> ready{false};
std::thread server(runMockServer, socketPath, std::ref(ready));

for (int i = 0; i < 50 && !ready.load(); ++i)
std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(20));

void *handle = dlopen(pluginPath.c_str(), RTLD_LAZY);
if (!handle) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] dlopen failed: " << dlerror() << "\n";
server.join();
return 1;
}

using CreateFn = PluginSystem::ATool *(*)();
auto *create = reinterpret_cast<CreateFn>(dlsym(handle, "instance"));
if (!create) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] dlsym failed: " << dlerror() << "\n";
dlclose(handle);
server.join();
return 1;
}

PluginSystem::ATool *tool = create();
if (!tool) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] instance returned null\n";
dlclose(handle);
server.join();
return 1;
}

std::string input = socketPath;

std::istringstream inputStream(input);
std::ostringstream outputStream;

auto *oldCin = std::cin.rdbuf(inputStream.rdbuf());
auto *oldCout = std::cout.rdbuf(outputStream.rdbuf());

tool->execute(nlohmann::json{});

std::cin.rdbuf(oldCin);
std::cout.rdbuf(oldCout);

delete tool;
dlclose(handle);

if (server.joinable())
server.join();

const std::string output = outputStream.str();
std::string outputLine = output;
std::size_t newline = outputLine.find('\n');
if (newline != std::string::npos)
outputLine.resize(newline);

std::cout << "[smoke] plugin output: " << outputLine << "\n";

if (output.find("\"ok\":true") == std::string::npos) {
std::cerr << "[smoke] Unexpected output: " << output << "\n";
return 1;
}

std::cout << "[smoke] OK\n";
return 0;
}

#include "tool-ping.hpp"

#include <cerrno>
#include <chrono>
#include <cstdio>

#include <poll.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>

#include <fcntl.h>
#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <sys/un.h>
#include <unistd.h>

namespace {
constexpr const char *PING_MARKER = "__AB_PING_OK__";

struct PingInput {
std::string socketPath;
std::string agentId;
int timeoutMs = 1000;
};

struct PingResult {
bool ok = false;
std::string error;
long long pingMs = 0;
};

std::string jsonEscape(const std::string &value)
{
std::string out;
out.reserve(value.size());
for (unsigned char c : value) {
switch (c) {
case '\\': out += "\\\\"; break;
case '"': out += "\\\""; break;
case '\n': out += "\\n"; break;
case '\r': out += "\\r"; break;
case '\t': out += "\\t"; break;
default:
if (c < 0x20) {
char buf[7];
std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "\\u%04x", c);
out += buf;
} else {
out += static_cast<char>(c);
}
}
}
return out;
}

std::string trim(const std::string &value)
{
const std::size_t first = value.find_first_not_of(" \t\r\n");
if (first == std::string::npos)
return "";
const std::size_t last = value.find_last_not_of(" \t\r\n");
return value.substr(first, last - first + 1);
}

bool parseInput(const std::string &input, PingInput &out, std::string &error)
{
out.socketPath = trim(input);
if (out.socketPath.empty()) {
error = "invalid_input";
return false;
}

return true;
}

bool pollReady(int fd, short events, int timeoutMs, std::string &error)
{
pollfd pfd{};
pfd.fd = fd;
pfd.events = events;
int rc = 0;
do {
rc = ::poll(&pfd, 1, timeoutMs);
} while (rc == -1 && errno == EINTR);
if (rc == 0) {
error = "timeout";
return false;
}
if (rc < 0) {
error = "poll_failed";
return false;
}
if (pfd.revents & events)
return true;
if (pfd.revents & (POLLERR | POLLHUP | POLLNVAL))
error = "poll_failed";
else
error = "poll_failed";
return false;
}

PingResult performPing(const PingInput &input)
{
PingResult result;
sockaddr_un addr{};
if (input.socketPath.size() >= sizeof(addr.sun_path)) {
result.error = "socket_path_too_long";
return result;
}

int fd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (fd == -1) {
result.error = "socket_create_failed";
return result;
}

int flags = ::fcntl(fd, F_GETFL, 0);
if (flags == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "socket_flags_failed";
return result;
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags | O_NONBLOCK) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "socket_flags_failed";
return result;
}

addr.sun_family = AF_UNIX;
std::snprintf(addr.sun_path, sizeof(addr.sun_path), "%s", input.socketPath.c_str());

int rc = ::connect(fd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), sizeof(addr));
if (rc == -1 && errno != EINPROGRESS) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

if (rc == -1) {
std::string pollError;
if (!pollReady(fd, POLLOUT, input.timeoutMs, pollError)) {
::close(fd);
result.error = (pollError == "timeout") ? "timeout" : "connect_failed";
return result;
}
int soError = 0;
socklen_t soLen = sizeof(soError);
if (::getsockopt(fd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, &soError, &soLen) == -1 || soError != 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags) == -1) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "socket_flags_failed";
return result;
}

std::string payload = std::string("echo ") + PING_MARKER + "\n";

auto start = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
std::size_t total = 0;
while (total < payload.size()) {
ssize_t sent = ::send(fd, payload.data() + total, payload.size() - total,
#ifdef MSG_NOSIGNAL
MSG_NOSIGNAL
#else
0
#endif
);
if (sent == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "send_failed";
return result;
}
total += static_cast<std::size_t>(sent);
}

const auto deadline = std::chrono::steady_clock::now()
+ std::chrono::milliseconds(input.timeoutMs);
std::string response;
char buffer[256];
while (std::chrono::steady_clock::now() < deadline) {
const auto remaining = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(
deadline - std::chrono::steady_clock::now()).count();
const int waitMs = remaining > 0 ? static_cast<int>(remaining) : 0;

std::string pollError;
if (!pollReady(fd, POLLIN, waitMs, pollError)) {
::close(fd);
result.error = (pollError == "timeout") ? "timeout" : "recv_failed";
return result;
}

ssize_t received = ::recv(fd, buffer, sizeof(buffer), 0);
if (received == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
::close(fd);
result.error = "recv_failed";
return result;
}
if (received == 0) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connection_closed";
return result;
}
response.append(buffer, static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
if (response.find(PING_MARKER) != std::string::npos)
break;
if (response.size() > 8192) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "invalid_response";
return result;
}
}

::close(fd);

auto end = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
if (response.find(PING_MARKER) == std::string::npos) {
result.error = "invalid_response";
return result;
}

result.ok = true;
result.pingMs = std::chrono::duration_cast<std::chrono::milliseconds>(end - start).count();
return result;
}

std::string buildOutput(const std::string &agentId,
bool ok,
const std::string &status,
const std::string &error,
const PingResult &result)
{
std::ostringstream out;
out << "{"
<< "\"type\":\"heartbeat\","
<< "\"agent_id\":\"" << jsonEscape(agentId) << "\","
<< "\"ok\":" << (ok ? "true" : "false") << ","
<< "\"status\":\"" << jsonEscape(status) << "\","
<< "\"ping_ms\":";
if (ok)
out << result.pingMs;
else
out << "null";
out << ",\"error\":";
if (ok)
out << "null";
else
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(error) << "\"";
out << "}";
return out.str();
}
}

void PluginSystem::ToolPing::toggle()
{
isActive = !isActive;
}

void PluginSystem::ToolPing::execute(const nlohmann::json &params)
{
PingInput input;
input.agentId = params.value("agent_id", std::string("shell"));
input.timeoutMs = params.value("timeout_ms", input.timeoutMs);

if (input.timeoutMs <= 0)
input.timeoutMs = 1000;

std::string error;

if (!isActive) {
const std::string output = buildOutput(
input.agentId,
false,
"OFFLINE",
"disabled",
PingResult{});
std::cout << output << std::endl;
return;
}

std::string inputLine;
if (!std::getline(std::cin, inputLine))
inputLine.clear();

if (!parseInput(inputLine, input, error)) {
const std::string output = buildOutput(
input.agentId,
false,
"OFFLINE",
error.empty() ? "invalid_input" : error,
PingResult{});
std::cout << output << std::endl;
return;
}

PingResult result = performPing(input);
if (result.ok) {
const std::string output = buildOutput(
input.agentId,
true,
"ALIVE",
"",
result);
std::cout << output << std::endl;
return;
}

const std::string output = buildOutput(
input.agentId,
false,
"OFFLINE",
result.error.empty() ? "unknown_error" : result.error,
result);
std::cout << output << std::endl;
}

extern "C" PluginSystem::ATool *instance(void)
{
return new PluginSystem::ToolPing();
}

namespace {
constexpr std::size_t MAX_LINE_SIZE = 4096;
constexpr const char *SYSINFO_MARKER = "AB_SYSINFO";

struct SysinfoInput {
std::string socketPath;
std::string agentId;
int timeoutMs = 1500;
};

struct SysinfoPayload {
double cpu = 0.0;
double ram = 0.0;
double disk = 0.0;
double uploadSpeed = 0.0;
double downloadSpeed = 0.0;
};

struct SysinfoResult {
bool ok = false;
std::string error;
SysinfoPayload payload;
};

enum class PollStatus {
READY,
TIMEOUT,
ERROR
};

std::string jsonEscape(const std::string &value)
{
std::string out;
out.reserve(value.size());
for (unsigned char c : value) {
switch (c) {
case '\\': out += "\\\\"; break;
case '"': out += "\\\""; break;
case '\n': out += "\\n"; break;
case '\r': out += "\\r"; break;
case '\t': out += "\\t"; break;
default:
if (c < 0x20) {
char buf[7];
std::snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), "\\u%04x", c);
out += buf;
} else {
out += static_cast<char>(c);
}
}
}
return out;
}

bool parseStrictFloat(const std::string &token, double &out)
{
errno = 0;
char *end = nullptr;
const char *begin = token.c_str();
double parsed = std::strtod(begin, &end);

if (begin == end || *end != '\0')
return false;
if (errno == ERANGE)
return false;
if (!std::isfinite(parsed))
return false;

out = parsed;
return true;
}

std::string trim(const std::string &value)
{
const std::size_t first = value.find_first_not_of(" \t\r\n");
if (first == std::string::npos)
return "";
const std::size_t last = value.find_last_not_of(" \t\r\n");
return value.substr(first, last - first + 1);
}

bool parseInput(const std::string &input, SysinfoInput &out)
{
out.socketPath = trim(input);
return !out.socketPath.empty();
}

PollStatus pollReady(int fd, short events, int timeoutMs)
{
pollfd pfd{};
pfd.fd = fd;
pfd.events = events;

int rc = 0;
do {
rc = ::poll(&pfd, 1, timeoutMs);
} while (rc == -1 && errno == EINTR);

if (rc == 0)
return PollStatus::TIMEOUT;
if (rc < 0)
return PollStatus::ERROR;
if (pfd.revents & events)
return PollStatus::READY;
return PollStatus::ERROR;
}

bool parseReply(const std::string &line, SysinfoPayload &payload)
{
std::istringstream stream(line);
std::vector<std::string> tokens;
std::string token;
while (stream >> token)
tokens.push_back(token);

if (tokens.size() != 6)
return false;
if (tokens[0] != SYSINFO_MARKER)
return false;

return parseStrictFloat(tokens[1], payload.cpu)
&& parseStrictFloat(tokens[2], payload.ram)
&& parseStrictFloat(tokens[3], payload.disk)
&& parseStrictFloat(tokens[4], payload.uploadSpeed)
&& parseStrictFloat(tokens[5], payload.downloadSpeed);
}

SysinfoResult requestSysinfo(const SysinfoInput &input)
{
SysinfoResult result;

sockaddr_un addr{};
if (input.socketPath.size() >= sizeof(addr.sun_path)) {
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

int fd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (fd == -1) {
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}

int flags = ::fcntl(fd, F_GETFL, 0);

result.error = "connect_failed";

}

if (rc == -1 && errno != EINPROGRESS) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";

}

if (rc == -1) {
const PollStatus status = pollReady(fd, POLLOUT, input.timeoutMs);

}
if (status == PollStatus::ERROR) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";

int soError = 0;

::close(fd);
result.error = "connect_failed";
return result;
}
}

if (::fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, flags) == -1) {

result.error = "connect_failed";

const std::string request =

"RAM=$(awk '/MemTotal:/{t=$2} /MemAvailable:/{a=$2} END{if (t>0) printf \"%.2f\", ((t-a)*100)/t; else printf \"0\"}' /proc/meminfo); "
"DISK=$(df -P / | awk 'NR==2{gsub(\"%\", \"\", $5); printf \"%.2f\", $5}'); "

+ std::string(SYSINFO_MARKER)
+ " $CPU $RAM $DISK 0 0\n";

if (responseBuffer.size() > MAX_LINE_SIZE) {
::close(fd);
result.error = "bad_reply";
return result;
}

std::size_t newlinePos = std::string::npos;
while ((newlinePos = responseBuffer.find('\n')) != std::string::npos) {
std::string line = responseBuffer.substr(0, newlinePos);
responseBuffer.erase(0, newlinePos + 1);

SysinfoPayload payload;
if (parseReply(line, payload)) {
::close(fd);
result.ok = true;
result.payload = payload;
return result;
}
}
}

::close(fd);
result.error = "bad_reply";
return result;
}

std::string buildOutput(const std::string &agentId, const SysinfoResult &result)
{
std::ostringstream out;
out.imbue(std::locale::classic());
out << std::setprecision(15);

out << "{"
<< "\"type\":\"sysinfo\"," << "\"agent_id\":\"" << jsonEscape(agentId) << "\","
<< "\"ok\":" << (result.ok ? "true" : "false") << ","
<< "\"cpu\":";

if (result.ok)
out << result.payload.cpu;
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"ram\":";
if (result.ok)
out << result.payload.ram;
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"disk\":";
if (result.ok)
out << result.payload.disk;
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"upload_speed\":";
if (result.ok)
out << result.payload.uploadSpeed;
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"download_speed\":";
if (result.ok)
out << result.payload.downloadSpeed;
else
out << "null";

out << ",\"error\":";
if (result.ok)
out << "null";
else
out << "\"" << jsonEscape(result.error.empty() ? "recv_failed" : result.error) << "\"";

out << "}";
return out.str();
}
}

void PluginSystem::ToolSysinfo::toggle()
{
isActive = !isActive;
}

void PluginSystem::ToolSysinfo::execute(const nlohmann::json &params)
{
SysinfoInput input;
input.agentId = params.value("agent_id", std::string("shell"));
input.timeoutMs = params.value("timeout_ms", input.timeoutMs);

if (input.timeoutMs <= 0)
input.timeoutMs = 1500;

std::string inputLine;
if (!std::getline(std::cin, inputLine))
inputLine.clear();

if (!parseInput(inputLine, input)) {
SysinfoResult result;
result.error = "parse_error";
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
return;
}

if (!isActive) {
SysinfoResult result;
result.error = "disabled";
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
return;
}

const SysinfoResult result = requestSysinfo(input);
std::cout << buildOutput(input.agentId, result) << std::endl;
}

{
return new PluginSystem::ToolSysinfo();
}

#include "CxxABI.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
std::string CxxABI::demangle(const char* name)
{
int   status = -4;
char* _out   = abi::__cxa_demangle(name, nullptr, nullptr, &status);
if (status != 0 && _out) free(_out);
if (status != 0) return std::string(name);
std::string result(_out);
free(_out);
return result;
}

std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos)
{
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> output;
std::string                         entry(symbolInfos);

std::size_t openP  = entry.find('(');
std::size_t closeP = entry.find(')');

if (openP == std::string::npos || closeP == std::string::npos)
return output;

std::string functionAndOffset = entry.substr(openP + 1, closeP);
std::size_t plusP             = functionAndOffset.find('+');

if (functionAndOffset.empty() || plusP == std::string::npos)
return output;

output.first =
CxxABI::demangle(functionAndOffset.substr(0, plusP).c_str());
std::stringstream ss;
ss << std::hex << functionAndOffset.substr(plusP + 1);
ss >> output.second;

return output;
}

// std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
// CxxABI::getBacktrace()
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> CxxABI::getBacktrace()
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> out;
int                                              TRACE_CHUNCK = 128;

int size = 0, out_size = 0;
do {
size += TRACE_CHUNCK;
void* trace[size + 1];
out_size = (int)backtrace(trace, size);
} while (out_size == size);
void* trace[out_size + 1];

backtrace(trace, out_size);
char** symbols = backtrace_symbols(trace, out_size);
if (symbols) {
for (int i = 0; i < out_size; ++i) {
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> symbol =
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(symbols[i]);
if (symbol.first.empty()) break;
out.push_back(symbol);
}
free(symbols);
}

return out;
}
}

#ifndef INCLUDED_CXXABI_HPP
#define INCLUDED_CXXABI_HPP

#include "../Headers.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
/**
     * Wrapper around usefull C++/C low level functions
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Got demangle, backtrace...
     *
     * @tip
     * This class is only made of static functions. It does not contain
     * constructor, nor destructor This means that you don't need to instanciate
     * it, and only use it as if it was a namespace.
     */
class CxxABI
{
public:
CxxABI() = delete;

/**
             * Demangle a C++ name.
             *
             * This is usefull for : Class names, backtrace...
             *
             * @return string containing : demangled name, or original name, if
             * demangle did not work
             */
static std::string demangle(const char* name);

/**
             * Get functions infos from symbol extraction
             *
             * @return A pair with the function names demangled, and the offset
             * from the function
             *
             * @warning
             * The function name is not EXACTLY the real name of the function.
             * Also, the offset DOES NOT correspond to the line number, but the
             * offset of asm instructions
             */
static std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos);

/**
             * Retreive the execution trace
             *
             * @return Vector of pair<pair<string, size_t>, string>. I'll
             * explain
             *
             * The idea is to retrieive the file, line, and function. So that's
             * what we do : pair or : (file, line), function
             *
             * @warning
             * To correctly grab function names, you will need to add
             * compilation flag "-rdynamic" See this lib's CMakeLists.txt for
             * implementation. This will export ALL symbols. If NOT done, you
             * will get offsets from binary. It's ok, but quite disgusting on
             * output.
             */
static std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>
getBacktrace();
};
}

#endif

#include "Exceptions.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
Exception::Exception(
const std::string&                                          what,
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> infos):
_infos(infos)
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> stackTrace =
CxxABI::getBacktrace();
std::stringstream ss;

ss << "[" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE
<< this->getClassNameFromStackTrace(stackTrace)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET;

if (!infos.first.first.empty() && !infos.second.empty()) {
ss << " in " << Flint::Colors::F_YELLOW << infos.second
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " << Flint::Colors::F_BLUE
<< infos.first.first.substr(
infos.first.first.find_last_of("/\\") + 1)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< infos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}
ss << "] " << what;

for (std::size_t i = 2; i < stackTrace.size(); i++) {
std::string funcName = stackTrace[i].first;
std::size_t openP    = funcName.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) funcName += "()";
funcName = funcName.substr(0, funcName.find("(") + 1) + "[...])";
ss << std::endl
<< " -> " << Flint::Colors::CYAN << funcName
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << "0x"
<< std::hex << stackTrace[i].second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}

this->_what = ss.str();
}

std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
Exception::getInfos() const
{
return this->_infos;
}

std::string Exception::getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>& stackTrace) const
{
if (stackTrace.size() <= 2) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string classNameSymbol = stackTrace[2].first.substr();
std::size_t openP           = classNameSymbol.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string functionName = classNameSymbol.substr(0, openP);
std::size_t lastColon    = functionName.rfind("::");
if (lastColon == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";
// Remove duplicate
return functionName.substr(0, lastColon);
}

std::string Exception::show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> fileInfos) const
{
std::stringstream ss;
ss << "Exception caught on " << Flint::Colors::F_GREEN
<< fileInfos.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << " at "
<< Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << fileInfos.first.first
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< fileInfos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl;

ss << this->_what;
return ss.str();
}

const char* Exception::what() const noexcept
{
return this->_what.c_str();
}

std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Exceptions::Exception& obj)
{
os << obj.what();
return os;
}
}

#ifndef INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP
#define INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP

#include "../Colors/Colors.hpp"
#include "../CxxABI/CxxABI.hpp"
#include "../Headers.hpp"
#include "../hpp/Preprocessor.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
/**
     * Wrapper arround default
     * [std::exception](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/error/exception)
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Store the the exception description as
     * [std::string](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/string/basic_string), and
     * store some usefull localisation informations: Filename, Line, and
     * Function Name. This is automated using the throw_exception preprocessor
     * directive.
     *
     * @tip
     * Take a look at the NotImplementedError to see how easy it is to implmeent
     * your own Flint::Exception.
     */
class Exception: public std::exception
{
protected:
std::string                                                 _what;
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> _infos;

public:
/**
             * Exception constructor. This need to be called, even on child
             * classes.
             *
             * @param what Define the exception's description
             * @param infos Might contain positional infos
             *
             * @warning
             * You should **NOT** use any excpetion constructor manually.
             * While they might work, the prefered way is to use the
             * throw_exception preprocessor directive. Using it will only
             * require you to register what type of excpetion you want to throw,
             * and what message you want to add, and will take care of putting
             * file name, line, and function name
             */
Exception(const std::string&     what  = "An exception occured !",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {
{"", 0},
""
});

/**
             * Retreive given infos (filename, line, and function name)
             *
             * @return The actual infos
             */
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
getInfos() const;

/**
             * Try to retreive the Exception class name from the StackTrace.
             *
             * @param stackTrace A stack trace, formated like
             * CxxABI::getBacktrace()
             *
             * @warning
             * This function **DOES NOT WORK 100% OF THE TIME**.
             * It is really dependant on the environement, and can return
             * undefined without aparent reason. It should **NOT** be used for
             * anything else than debug or display.
             *
             * @return The actual class name or "[Class name undefined]"
             */
std::string getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>&
stackTrace) const;

/**
             * Display the error, given specific positional infos (like the
             * constructor).
             *
             * @param infos Positional informations, like the constructor, used
             * to display the infos.
             *
             * @note
             * This function, while being usable without, should be used through
             * the catch_exception preprocessor directive. This preprocessor
             * directive let you display Python like excpetion trace.
             *
             * @return The formated exception, ready to be displayed.
             */
std::string show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos) const;

/**
             * Required override, deprecated by show and catch_exception.
             *
             * @info
             * This is the required std::exception override.
             * It'll return the _what as a const char*.
             * While this will work, prefer using the show function or the
             * catch_excpetion preprocessor directive :
             *
             * @example
             * ```cpp
             * try {
             *     throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::Exception, "Sample testing
             * exception"); } catch (const Flint::Exceptions::Exception& e) {
             *     std::cerr << catch_exception(e) << std::endl;
             * }
             * ```
             *
             * @return The _what as a C string
             */
const char* what() const noexcept override;

/**
             * operator<< override
             *
             * @abstract
             * This will simply append the _what to the ostream
             */
friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&                os,
const Exceptions::Exception& obj);
};

/**
     * Sample Flint::Exceptions::Exception to register NotImplemented features
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * @tip
     * You should use that Exception as a C++ Implementation of the Rust todo!()
     * macro.
     *
     * @example
     * ```cpp
     * throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::NotImplementedError, "This function
     * haven't been implemented as of today !");
     * ```
     */
class NotImplementedError: public Flint::Exceptions::Exception
{
public:
NotImplementedError(const std::string& what =
"Asked action haven't been implemented yet.",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {{"", 0}, ""}):
Exception(what, infos)
{
}
};
}

#endif

#include "Init.hpp"

namespace Flint

static void unhandeled_exception()
{
try {
std::rethrow_exception(std::current_exception());
} catch (const std::exception& e) {
const Flint::Exceptions::Exception* exception =
dynamic_cast<const Flint::Exceptions::Exception*>(&e);
if (exception != nullptr) {
std::cerr << Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " Uncaught Flint exception " << Flint::Colors::RED
<< "/!\\" << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl
<< *exception << std::endl;
} else {
std::cerr << Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " Uncaught exception "
<< Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl
<< e.what() << std::endl;
}

void Logging::display_positional_infos(
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
positional_infos)
{
std::cout << "(at " << Flint::Colors::CYAN
<< positional_infos.first.first << Flint::Colors::RESET << ":"
<< Flint::Colors::PURPLE << positional_infos.first.second
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " in " << Flint::Colors::BLUE
<< positional_infos.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << ") ";
}

void Logging::debug(
const std::string& message,
std::optional<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
positional_infos)
{
if (Logging::get().display_level > Flint::LogLevel::DEBUG) return;

auto out = (Logging::get().log_on_stderr ? &std::cerr : &std::cout);

*out << "[ " << Flint::Colors::GREY << "DEBG" << Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " ] ";

if (positional_infos)
Logging::display_positional_infos(*positional_infos);

*out << message << std::endl;

auto backtrace = Flint::CxxABI::getBacktrace();

for (auto entry: backtrace)
*out << "    in " << Flint::Colors::PURPLE << entry.first
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::GREEN
<< entry.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl;
*out << "== End Debug Backtrace ==" << std::endl;

if (Logging::get().error_level <= Flint::LogLevel::DEBUG)
throw_exception(Flint::TooHighLevelLoggingError, message);
}

#ifndef INCLUDED_INSPECTION_HPP
#define INCLUDED_INSPECTION_HPP

#include "../CxxABI/CxxABI.hpp"
#include "../Headers.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
/**
     * Base class to implement simplet self-reflexion in C++
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * @tparam T Child class itself
     *
     * @tip
     * When creating a child class, define as follow :
     * ```cpp
     * class ChildClass: public Inspection<ChildClass>
     * ```
     * This will extand the ChildClass with the self-reflexion class Inspection
     *
     * @warning
     * If the class you want to extand as a reflected class will be a parent
     * class (I.E. You will create childs of this "target" class), you might
     * want to mark inheritence as **virtual**. This is needed if your child
     * class _also_ need to be reflected. This way, there is no confilict
     * between the parent reflexion, and the child reflexion.
     */
template<typename T>
class Inspection
{
public:
/**
             * Return the name of the reflected class
             *
             * @return The demangled name of the class, or just the name, if
             * demangleing failed
             */
virtual std::string getClassName() const final
{
return CxxABI::demangle(typeid(T).name());
}

// virtual std::unordered_set<std::pair<std::string, std::string>>
// getAttributes();

/**
             * Return a string representation of the class
             *
             * @tip
             * This function is not intended to be used.
             * This is because this functions display the reflection.
             * You might prefer to override this function in yout child
             * class to display informations that are more relevant to
             * your specific class.
             *
             * @example
             * You have a Macro available to make the str function really
             * easilly :
             * ```cpp
             * display_attr(attribute)
             * ```
             * Which will automaticly translate to
             * ```cpp
             * Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << "attribute" << Flint::Colors::RESET <<
             * "=" << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute <<
             * Flint::Colors::RESET
             * ```
             * There is also a `make_str` macro, which you can use as follow :
             * ```cpp
             * return make_str("a=12" << ", " << "b=5");
             * ```
             * that becomes
             * ```cpp
             * return ({std::stringstream ss; ss << "<" << this->getClassName()
             * << " at " << std::hex << this << ": " << "a=12" << ", " << "b=5"
             * << ">"; ss.str()})
             * ```
             * It means you can define the str function of your class as like
             * that :
             * ```cpp
             * // Provided you have 3 attributes called a, b and c
             * return make_str(display_attr(a) << ", " << display_attr(b) << ",
             * " << display_attr(c));
             * ```
             *
             * @return The string representation of the class
             */
virtual std::string str() const
{
std::stringstream ss;

ss << "<" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE << this->getClassName()
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " << Flint::Colors::YELLOW
<< std::hex << this << Flint::Colors::RESET << ">";

return ss.str();
}

/** Overload the operator << to use the str method of the object
             *
             * @tparam T Reflected class
             *
             * @param os Reference of ostream (I.E. std::cout, std::cerr...)
             *
             * @param obj Object reference to be displayed
             *
             * @return Param os
             */
friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&        os,
const Inspection<T>& obj)
{
os << obj.str();
return os;
}
};
}


#endif

#ifndef INCLUDED_PREPROCESSOR_HPP
#define INCLUDED_PREPROCESSOR_HPP

#if defined(__GNUC__)
#define __FUNCTION__ __PRETTY_FUNCTION__
#elif defined(_MSC_VER)
#define __FUNCTION__ __FUNCSIG__
#else
#define __FUNCTION__ __func__
#endif

#define __POSITION_INFOS__                                                    \
    std::pair                                                                 \
    {                                                                         \
        std::pair{std::string(__FILE__), __LINE__}, std::string(__FUNCTION__) \
    }

#define throw_exception(exception, message)       \
    throw(exception(message, __POSITION_INFOS__))
#define catch_exception(exception) exception.show(__POSITION_INFOS__)

// Inspection
#define display_attr(attribute)                                        \
    Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << #attribute << Flint::Colors::RESET << "=" \
                          << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute     \
                          << Flint::Colors::RESET
#define make_str(display)                                            \
    ({                                                               \
        std::stringstream ss;                                        \
        ss << Flint::Colors::RESET << "<" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE \
           << this->getClassName() << Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " \
           << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << std::hex << this << std::dec  \
           << Flint::Colors::RESET << ": " << display << ">";        \
        ss.str();                                                    \
    })

#define log_debug(message)                                                 \
    Flint::Logging::debug(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_info(message)  Flint::Logging::info(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_warn(message)  Flint::Logging::warning(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_error(message) Flint::Logging::error(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_critical(message)                             \
    Flint::Logging::critical(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))

#endif

/*
** EPITECH PROJECT, 2025
** tek4
** File description:
** UnixSessionManager
*/

#include "UnixSessionManager.hpp"

#include <cerrno>
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstring>
#include <poll.h>
#include <stdexcept>

#include <sys/socket.h>
#include <sys/un.h>
#include <unistd.h>

namespace PluginSystem
{
UnixSessionManager::UnixSessionManager(std::string listenerName, std::filesystem::path rootDir)
: _listenerName(std::move(listenerName))
, _rootDir(std::move(rootDir))
, _sessions()
, _activeSession()
{
if (_listenerName.empty())
throw std::invalid_argument("UnixSessionManager requires a listener name");
if (_rootDir.empty())
throw std::invalid_argument("UnixSessionManager requires a root directory");
}

UnixSessionManager::~UnixSessionManager()
{
closeAllSessions();
}

std::filesystem::path UnixSessionManager::createSession(const std::string &sessionId, int backlog)
{
if (sessionId.empty())
throw std::invalid_argument("Session id cannot be empty");
if (backlog <= 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("Session backlog must be > 0");

ensureDirectoryExists();
closeSession(sessionId);

const auto path = getSessionPath(sessionId);
::unlink(path.c_str());

int fd = ::socket(AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
if (fd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error("Unable to create AF_UNIX socket: "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));

sockaddr_un addr{};
addr.sun_family = AF_UNIX;
std::snprintf(addr.sun_path, sizeof(addr.sun_path), "%s", path.c_str());

if (::bind(fd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), sizeof(addr)) == -1) {
::close(fd);
throw std::runtime_error("bind failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}

if (::listen(fd, backlog) == -1) {
::close(fd);
throw std::runtime_error("listen failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}

_sessions.emplace(sessionId, SessionChannel{path, fd, -1});
return path;
}

void UnixSessionManager::closeSession(const std::string &sessionId)
{
auto it = _sessions.find(sessionId);
if (it == _sessions.end())
return;

if (it->second.clientFd != -1) {
::close(it->second.clientFd);
it->second.clientFd = -1;
}
if (it->second.listenFd != -1) {
::close(it->second.listenFd);
it->second.listenFd = -1;
}
if (!it->second.path.empty()) {
std::error_code ec;
std::filesystem::remove(it->second.path, ec);
}
_sessions.erase(it);

if (_activeSession == sessionId)
_activeSession.clear();
}

void UnixSessionManager::closeAllSessions()
{
for (auto &entry: _sessions) {
if (entry.second.clientFd != -1)
::close(entry.second.clientFd);
if (entry.second.listenFd != -1)
::close(entry.second.listenFd);
if (!entry.second.path.empty()) {
std::error_code ec;
std::filesystem::remove(entry.second.path, ec);
}
}
_sessions.clear();
_activeSession.clear();
}

void UnixSessionManager::setActiveSession(const std::string &sessionId)
{
if (_sessions.find(sessionId) == _sessions.end())
createSession(sessionId);
_activeSession = sessionId;
}

std::filesystem::path UnixSessionManager::getSessionPath(const std::string &sessionId) const
{
return _rootDir / _listenerName / ("session_" + sessionId + ".sock");
}

void UnixSessionManager::writeActive(const std::string &data)
{
if (_activeSession.empty())
throw std::runtime_error("No active session selected");
writeSession(_activeSession, data);
}

std::string UnixSessionManager::readActive(std::size_t maxBytes, bool nonBlocking)
{
if (_activeSession.empty())
throw std::runtime_error("No active session selected");
return readSession(_activeSession, maxBytes, nonBlocking);
}

void UnixSessionManager::writeSession(const std::string &sessionId, const std::string &data)
{
auto &channel = requireConnectedSession(sessionId);
const char *buffer = data.data();
std::size_t total  = 0;
const std::size_t size = data.size();

while (total < size) {
ssize_t sent = ::send(channel.clientFd, buffer + total, size - total,
#ifdef MSG_NOSIGNAL
MSG_NOSIGNAL
#else
0
#endif
);
if (sent == -1) {
if (errno == EINTR)
continue;
if (errno == EPIPE || errno == ECONNRESET) {
::close(channel.clientFd);
channel.clientFd = -1;
}
throw std::runtime_error(
"write failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}
total += static_cast<std::size_t>(sent);
}
}

std::string UnixSessionManager::readSession(const std::string &sessionId,
std::size_t maxBytes,
bool        nonBlocking)
{
if (maxBytes == 0)
return {};

auto &channel = requireConnectedSession(sessionId, nonBlocking);
if (channel.clientFd == -1)
return {};

std::string buffer(maxBytes, '\0');
const int   flags = nonBlocking ? MSG_DONTWAIT : 0;

ssize_t received = ::recv(channel.clientFd, buffer.data(), buffer.size(), flags);
if (received == -1) {
if (nonBlocking && (errno == EWOULDBLOCK || errno == EAGAIN))
return {};
if (errno == ECONNRESET) {
::close(channel.clientFd);
channel.clientFd = -1;
return {};
}
throw std::runtime_error(
"read failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}
if (received == 0) {
::close(channel.clientFd);
channel.clientFd = -1;
return {};
}

buffer.resize(static_cast<std::size_t>(received));
return buffer;
}

UnixSessionManager::SessionChannel &
UnixSessionManager::requireSession(const std::string &sessionId)
{
auto it = _sessions.find(sessionId);
if (it == _sessions.end())
throw std::runtime_error("Unknown session id: " + sessionId);
return it->second;
}

UnixSessionManager::SessionChannel &
UnixSessionManager::requireConnectedSession(const std::string &sessionId, bool nonBlocking)
{
auto &channel = requireSession(sessionId);
if (channel.clientFd != -1)
return channel;

if (nonBlocking) {
pollfd pfd{};
pfd.fd = channel.listenFd;
pfd.events = POLLIN;
int pollResult = 0;
do {
pollResult = ::poll(&pfd, 1, 0);
} while (pollResult == -1 && errno == EINTR);

if (pollResult == 0)
return channel;
if (pollResult < 0)
throw std::runtime_error(
"poll failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));
}

sockaddr_un addr{};
socklen_t   len = sizeof(addr);
int         fd  = ::accept(channel.listenFd, reinterpret_cast<sockaddr*>(&addr), &len);
if (fd == -1)
throw std::runtime_error(
"accept failed for session " + sessionId + ": "
+ std::string(std::strerror(errno)));

channel.clientFd = fd;
return channel;
}

void UnixSessionManager::ensureDirectoryExists() const
{
const auto dir = _rootDir / _listenerName;
if (!std::filesystem::exists(dir))
std::filesystem::create_directories(dir);
}
}

/*

** tek4

** UnixSessionManager
*/

#ifndef UNIXSESSIONMANAGER_HPP_
#define UNIXSESSIONMANAGER_HPP_

#include <cstddef>
#include <filesystem>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace PluginSystem
{
class UnixSessionManager
{
public:
UnixSessionManager(std::string listenerName,
std::filesystem::path rootDir = "/tmp/listeners");
~UnixSessionManager();

std::filesystem::path createSession(const std::string &sessionId, int backlog = 1);
void                 closeSession(const std::string &sessionId);
void                 closeAllSessions();

void        setActiveSession(const std::string &sessionId);
const std::string &getActiveSession() const { return _activeSession; }
std::filesystem::path getSessionPath(const std::string &sessionId) const;

void        writeActive(const std::string &data);
std::string readActive(std::size_t maxBytes = 4096, bool nonBlocking = false);

void        writeSession(const std::string &sessionId, const std::string &data);
std::string readSession(const std::string &sessionId,
std::size_t maxBytes = 4096,
bool        nonBlocking = false);

private:
struct SessionChannel
{
std::filesystem::path path;
int                   listenFd;
int                   clientFd;
};

SessionChannel &requireSession(const std::string &sessionId);
SessionChannel &requireConnectedSession(const std::string &sessionId,
bool nonBlocking = false);
void            ensureDirectoryExists() const;

std::string                                _listenerName;
std::filesystem::path                      _rootDir;
std::unordered_map<std::string, SessionChannel> _sessions;
std::string                                _activeSession;
};
}
