#include "ListenerBridge.hpp"

#include <algorithm>
#include <chrono>
#include <exception>
#include <iostream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <thread>

namespace ListenerBridgeApp
{
namespace
{
bool isTcpBindPeerDisconnect(const std::exception &error)
{
const std::string message = error.what();
return message.find("TCPBindProtocol peer disconnected") != std::string::npos;
}
}

ListenerBridge::ListenerBridge(ListenerBridgeConfig config,
std::unique_ptr<PluginSystem::AProtocol> protocol)
: _config(std::move(config))
, _sessionManager(_config.listenerName, _config.sessionsRootDir)
, _protocol(std::move(protocol))
, _unixReadBuffer()
, _protocolReadBuffer()
, _pendingResponses(0)
, _protocolReady(false)
, _stopRequested(false)
, _reconnectDelay(_config.reconnectInitialMs)
, _nextReconnectAttempt(std::chrono::steady_clock::now())
{
if (_config.sessionId.empty())
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge requires a non-empty session id");
if (_config.unixReadMaxBytes == 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge unixReadMaxBytes must be > 0");
if (_config.protocolReadMaxBytes == 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge protocolReadMaxBytes must be > 0");
if (_config.idleSleepMs <= 0)
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge idleSleepMs must be > 0");
if (_config.reconnectInitialMs <= 0 || _config.reconnectMaxMs < _config.reconnectInitialMs)
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge reconnect settings are invalid");
if (!_protocol)
throw std::invalid_argument("ListenerBridge requires a protocol instance");

std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] IPC socket: "
<< _sessionManager.getSessionPath(_config.sessionId) << '\n';
}

void ListenerBridge::run()
{
while (!_stopRequested) {
bool didWork = false;

if (!ensureProtocolConnected()) {
std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(_config.idleSleepMs));
continue;
}

try {
didWork = pumpUnixToProtocol() || didWork;
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
handleProtocolError("forwarding unix->protocol", error);
}

try {
didWork = pumpProtocolToUnix() || didWork;
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
if (isTcpBindPeerDisconnect(error)) {
try {
_sessionManager.closeSession(_config.sessionId);
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] TCP client disconnected, session socket removed" << '\n';
} catch (const std::exception &sessionError) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Failed to remove session socket after client disconnect: "
<< sessionError.what() << '\n';
}
_protocolReadBuffer.clear();
_pendingResponses = 0;
} else {
handleProtocolError("forwarding protocol->unix", error);
}
}

if (!didWork)
std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(_config.idleSleepMs));
}

try {
_protocol->disconnect();
_protocolReady = false;
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Protocol disconnect failed: " << error.what() << '\n';
}
}

void ListenerBridge::requestStop()
{
_stopRequested = true;
}

bool ListenerBridge::ensureProtocolConnected()
{
if (_protocolReady)
return true;

const auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
if (now < _nextReconnectAttempt)
return false;

try {
_protocol->connect();
_protocolReady = true;
_reconnectDelay = std::chrono::milliseconds(_config.reconnectInitialMs);
_nextReconnectAttempt = now;
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Protocol connected" << '\n';
return true;
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Protocol connect failed: " << error.what()
<< " (retry in " << _reconnectDelay.count() << "ms)\n";
_nextReconnectAttempt = now + _reconnectDelay;
_reconnectDelay = std::min(
std::chrono::milliseconds(_config.reconnectMaxMs),
_reconnectDelay * 2);
return false;
}
}

void ListenerBridge::handleProtocolError(const std::string &context, const std::exception &error)
{
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Error while " << context << ": " << error.what() << '\n';

try {
_protocol->disconnect();
_protocolReady = false;
} catch (const std::exception &disconnectError) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Protocol disconnect failed: "
<< disconnectError.what() << '\n';
}

const auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
_nextReconnectAttempt = now + _reconnectDelay;
_reconnectDelay = std::min(
std::chrono::milliseconds(_config.reconnectMaxMs),
_reconnectDelay * 2);
_pendingResponses = 0;
_protocolReadBuffer.clear();

try {
_sessionManager.closeSession(_config.sessionId);
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Session socket removed after protocol disconnect" << '\n';
} catch (const std::exception &sessionError) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Failed to remove session socket: "
<< sessionError.what() << '\n';
}
}

bool ListenerBridge::pumpUnixToProtocol()
{
if (_sessionManager.getActiveSession().empty())
return false;

std::string chunk = _sessionManager.readActive(_config.unixReadMaxBytes, true);
if (!chunk.empty())
_unixReadBuffer += chunk;

bool didWork = false;
std::string line;
while (popLine(_unixReadBuffer, line)) {
_protocol->send(line + "\n");
++_pendingResponses;
didWork = true;
}
return didWork;
}

bool ListenerBridge::pumpProtocolToUnix()
{
std::string chunk = _protocol->receive(_config.protocolReadMaxBytes, true);
if (!chunk.empty())
_protocolReadBuffer += chunk;

// For listener protocols like tcp_bind, a client can connect without
// sending a newline-terminated payload yet. Create the unix session
// socket as soon as protocol reports an active connection.
if (_sessionManager.getActiveSession().empty() && _protocol->isConnected()) {
_sessionManager.setActiveSession(_config.sessionId);
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Session socket created on protocol client connect" << '\n';
}

bool didWork = false;
std::string line;
while (popLine(_protocolReadBuffer, line)) {
if (_sessionManager.getActiveSession().empty()) {
_sessionManager.setActiveSession(_config.sessionId);
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Session socket created on incoming protocol traffic" << '\n';
}
_sessionManager.writeActive(line + "\n");
if (_pendingResponses > 0)
--_pendingResponses;
didWork = true;
}
return didWork;
}

bool ListenerBridge::popLine(std::string &buffer, std::string &line)
{
std::size_t newlinePos = buffer.find('\n');
if (newlinePos == std::string::npos)
return false;

line = buffer.substr(0, newlinePos);
buffer.erase(0, newlinePos + 1);
return true;
}
}

#include "ListenerBridge.hpp"

#include <atomic>
#include <csignal>
#include <cstdint>
#include <dlfcn.h>
#include <exception>
#include <filesystem>
#include <iostream>
#include <memory>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

#include "PluginSystem/HotReloadSystem.hpp"
#include "json/json.hpp"

namespace {
struct CliOptions
{
std::string listenerName = "default";
std::string sessionId;
std::string sessionsRootDir = "/sessions";
std::string protocolsDir = "/plugin/protocols";
std::string proto;
std::string host;
std::string bindHost;
std::uint16_t port = 0;
};

struct ProtocolRuntime
{
std::unique_ptr<PluginSystem::AProtocol> protocol;
void *pluginHandle = nullptr;
};

using CreateProtocolFn = PluginSystem::AProtocol* (*)(const char *configJson);

static ListenerBridgeApp::ListenerBridge *g_bridge = nullptr;
static std::atomic<bool> g_stopRequested{false};
static std::atomic<bool> g_reloadRequested{false};

class ProtocolHotReloadWatcher: public HotReloadSystem
{
public:
ProtocolHotReloadWatcher(const std::string &directory,
const std::vector<std::string> &watchedNames)
: HotReloadSystem(directory)
, _watchedNames(watchedNames.begin(), watchedNames.end())
{
}

protected:
void onFileCreated(const std::string &filename) override
{
triggerReload(filename, "created");
}

void onFileModified(const std::string &filename) override
{
triggerReload(filename, "modified");
}

void onFileDeleted(const std::string &filename) override
{
triggerReload(filename, "deleted");
}

private:
void triggerReload(const std::string &filename, const char *event)
{
if (_watchedNames.find(filename) == _watchedNames.end())
return;

std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Protocol plugin file " << event
<< ": " << filename << ", scheduling reload\n";

g_reloadRequested = true;
if (g_bridge)
g_bridge->requestStop();
}

std::unordered_set<std::string> _watchedNames;
};

void printUsage(const char *binary)
{
std::cout
<< "Usage:\n"
<< "  " << binary << " --session <id> --proto <protocol-plugin-name> [options]\n\n"
<< "Options:\n"
<< "  --listener <name>     Listener namespace for <sessions-root>/<name>/... (default: default)\n"
<< "  --session <id>        Session id used for session_<id>.sock (required)\n"
<< "  --sessions-root <dir> Root directory for unix socket sessions (default: /sessions)\n"
<< "  --protocols-dir <dir> Directory containing protocol .so plugins (default: /plugin/protocols)\n"
<< "  --proto <name>        Protocol plugin name, e.g. tcp-bind or tcp_bind (required)\n"
<< "  --host <ip>           Host value passed to protocol config\n"
<< "  --bind <ip>           Bind value passed to protocol config\n"
<< "  --port <port>         Port for selected protocol\n"
<< "  --help                Show this help\n";
}

bool parsePort(const std::string &value, std::uint16_t &outPort)
{
try {
std::size_t idx = 0;
unsigned long parsed = std::stoul(value, &idx);
if (idx != value.size() || parsed == 0 || parsed > 65535)
return false;
outPort = static_cast<std::uint16_t>(parsed);
return true;
} catch (...) {
return false;
}
}

bool readArgValue(int argc, char **argv, int &index, std::string &out)
{
if (index + 1 >= argc)
return false;
++index;
out = argv[index];
return true;
}

bool parseArgs(int argc, char **argv, CliOptions &options)
{
for (int i = 1; i < argc; ++i) {
std::string arg = argv[i];
if (arg == "--help") {
printUsage(argv[0]);
return false;
}
if (arg == "--listener") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.listenerName))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --listener");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--session") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.sessionId))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --session");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--sessions-root") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.sessionsRootDir))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --sessions-root");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--protocols-dir") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.protocolsDir))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --protocols-dir");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--proto") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.proto))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --proto");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--host") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.host))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --host");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--bind") {
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, options.bindHost))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --bind");
continue;
}
if (arg == "--port") {
std::string portString;
if (!readArgValue(argc, argv, i, portString))
throw std::runtime_error("Missing value for --port");
if (!parsePort(portString, options.port))
throw std::runtime_error("Invalid --port value: " + portString);
continue;
}
throw std::runtime_error("Unknown argument: " + arg);
}

if (options.sessionId.empty())
throw std::runtime_error("--session is required");
if (options.proto.empty())
throw std::runtime_error("--proto is required");
if (options.port == 0)
throw std::runtime_error("--port is required and must be > 0");

if (options.host.empty())
options.host = "0.0.0.0";
if (options.bindHost.empty())
options.bindHost = "0.0.0.0";

return true;
}

std::string toDashed(std::string value)
{
for (auto &ch : value) {
if (ch == '_')
ch = '-';
}
return value;
}

std::string toUnderscored(std::string value)
{
for (auto &ch : value) {
if (ch == '-')
ch = '_';
}
return value;
}

std::vector<std::string> protocolLibraryCandidates(const std::string &name)
{
std::vector<std::string> candidates;

const std::string dashed = toDashed(name);
const std::string underscored = toUnderscored(name);
const std::string protocolSuffix = dashed + "-protocol";

auto pushUnique = [&candidates](const std::string &value) {
if (value.empty())
return;
if (std::find(candidates.begin(), candidates.end(), value) == candidates.end())
candidates.push_back(value);
};

pushUnique(name);
pushUnique(dashed);
pushUnique(underscored);
pushUnique(protocolSuffix);

std::vector<std::string> asFiles;
asFiles.reserve(candidates.size() * 2);
for (const auto &candidate : candidates) {
asFiles.push_back(candidate);
asFiles.push_back(candidate + ".so");
}

return asFiles;
}

nlohmann::json protocolConfigJson(const CliOptions &options)
{
const std::string protoKey = toUnderscored(options.proto);
const std::string host = protoKey == "tcp_bind" ? options.bindHost : options.host;
return nlohmann::json{
{"host", host},
{"bind", options.bindHost},
{"port", options.port}
};
}

ProtocolRuntime createProtocol(const CliOptions &options)
{
namespace fs = std::filesystem;

const fs::path protocolsPath(options.protocolsDir);
const auto candidates = protocolLibraryCandidates(options.proto);
const std::string configDump = protocolConfigJson(options).dump();

for (const auto &candidate : candidates) {
const fs::path libraryPath = protocolsPath / candidate;
if (!fs::exists(libraryPath) || !fs::is_regular_file(libraryPath))
continue;

void *handle = ::dlopen(libraryPath.string().c_str(), RTLD_NOW);
if (!handle) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] dlopen failed for " << libraryPath
<< ": " << ::dlerror() << '\n';
continue;
}

::dlerror();
auto createFn = reinterpret_cast<CreateProtocolFn>(::dlsym(handle, "create_protocol"));
const char *createError = ::dlerror();
if (createError) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] Missing create_protocol in " << libraryPath
<< ": " << createError << '\n';
::dlclose(handle);
continue;
}

PluginSystem::AProtocol *raw = nullptr;
try {
raw = createFn(configDump.c_str());
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] create_protocol threw for " << libraryPath
<< ": " << error.what() << '\n';
::dlclose(handle);
continue;
}

if (!raw) {
std::cerr << "[ListenerBridge] create_protocol returned null for "
<< libraryPath << '\n';
::dlclose(handle);
continue;
}

std::unique_ptr<PluginSystem::AProtocol> protocol(raw);
protocol->toggle();

std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Loaded protocol plugin: " << libraryPath << '\n';

ProtocolRuntime runtime;
runtime.protocol = std::move(protocol);
runtime.pluginHandle = handle;
return runtime;
}

throw std::runtime_error(
"Unable to load protocol plugin for '" + options.proto +
"' from directory: " + options.protocolsDir);
}

extern "C" void handleSignal(int)
{
g_stopRequested = true;
if (g_bridge)
g_bridge->requestStop();
}
}

int main(int argc, char **argv)
{
CliOptions options;

try {
if (!parseArgs(argc, argv, options))
return 0;

ListenerBridgeApp::ListenerBridgeConfig bridgeConfig;
bridgeConfig.listenerName = options.listenerName;
bridgeConfig.sessionId = options.sessionId;
bridgeConfig.sessionsRootDir = options.sessionsRootDir;

std::signal(SIGINT, handleSignal);
std::signal(SIGTERM, handleSignal);

const auto watchCandidates = protocolLibraryCandidates(options.proto);
ProtocolHotReloadWatcher hotReload(options.protocolsDir, watchCandidates);
hotReload.start();

while (!g_stopRequested) {
auto runtime = createProtocol(options);
ListenerBridgeApp::ListenerBridge bridge(bridgeConfig, std::move(runtime.protocol));
g_bridge = &bridge;

std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Started with protocol=" << options.proto
<< ", listener=" << options.listenerName
<< ", session=" << options.sessionId
<< ", port=" << options.port
<< '\n';

bridge.run();
g_bridge = nullptr;

if (runtime.pluginHandle)
::dlclose(runtime.pluginHandle);

if (g_reloadRequested.exchange(false)) {
std::cout << "[ListenerBridge] Reloading protocol plugin...\n";
continue;
}

if (!g_stopRequested)
break;
}

hotReload.stop();

return 0;
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
std::cerr << "listener-bridge error: " << error.what() << '\n';
printUsage(argv[0]);
return 1;
}
}

#include "PluginSystem/PluginLoader.hpp"
#include "PluginSystem/PluginHotloader.hpp"   // ta version qui hérite de HotReloadSystem
#include "PluginSystem/ToolScheduler.hpp"
#include <csignal>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>
#include <iostream>

static volatile std::sig_atomic_t g_stopRequested = 0;
extern "C" void handle_signal(int) { g_stopRequested = 1; }

int main()
{
std::signal(SIGINT, handle_signal);
std::signal(SIGTERM, handle_signal);

std::vector<PluginSystem::ATool*> tools;
std::vector<PluginSystem::AProtocol*> protocols;

PluginSystem::PluginLoader loader(tools, protocols);

const std::string pluginsDir = "/plugins/";
loader.loadAllPlugins(pluginsDir);

PluginSystem::PluginHotloader hotloader(pluginsDir, loader);
hotloader.start();

PluginSystem::ToolScheduler scheduler(tools);
scheduler.start();

std::cout << "[Main] Press Ctrl+C to stop..." << std::endl;

while (!g_stopRequested) {
std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(250));
}

scheduler.stop();
hotloader.stop();

return 0;
}

#include "HotReloadSystem.hpp"
#include <sys/inotify.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <iostream>
#include <chrono>
#include <thread>
#include <cerrno>

HotReloadSystem::HotReloadSystem(const std::string &directory)
: _directory(directory)
{
}

HotReloadSystem::~HotReloadSystem()
{
stop();
}

void HotReloadSystem::start()
{
if (_running)
return;
_running = true;
_watcherThread = std::thread(&HotReloadSystem::watchLoop, this);
}

void HotReloadSystem::stop()
{
if (!_running)
return;
_running = false;

if (_inotifyFd >= 0) {
if (_watchDescriptor >= 0)
inotify_rm_watch(_inotifyFd, _watchDescriptor);
close(_inotifyFd);
_inotifyFd = -1;
}

if (_watcherThread.joinable())
_watcherThread.join();
}

void HotReloadSystem::watchLoop()
{
_inotifyFd = inotify_init1(IN_NONBLOCK);
if (_inotifyFd < 0) {
std::cerr << "[HotReloadSystem] Failed to init inotify\n";
return;
}

_watchDescriptor = inotify_add_watch(
_inotifyFd,
_directory.c_str(),
IN_CREATE | IN_MODIFY | IN_DELETE | IN_MOVED_TO | IN_MOVED_FROM
);

if (_watchDescriptor < 0) {
std::cerr << "[HotReloadSystem] Failed to watch directory: " << _directory << "\n";
close(_inotifyFd);
_inotifyFd = -1;
return;
}

std::cout << "[HotReloadSystem] Watching directory: " << _directory << "\n";

const size_t eventSize = sizeof(struct inotify_event);
const size_t bufLen = 1024 * (eventSize + 16);
char buffer[bufLen];

while (_running) {
int length = read(_inotifyFd, buffer, bufLen);
if (length < 0) {
if (errno == EAGAIN) {
std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(100));
continue;
}
break;
}

int i = 0;
while (i < length) {
struct inotify_event *event = reinterpret_cast<struct inotify_event*>(&buffer[i]);
if (event->len > 0) {
std::string filename = event->name;
if (event->mask & (IN_CREATE | IN_MOVED_TO)) {
onFileCreated(filename);
} else if (event->mask & IN_MODIFY) {
onFileModified(filename);
} else if (event->mask & (IN_DELETE | IN_MOVED_FROM)) {
onFileDeleted(filename);
}
}
i += eventSize + event->len;
}
}

std::cout << "[HotReloadSystem] Stopped watching.\n";
}

#include "PluginHotloader.hpp"
#include <filesystem>
#include <iostream>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>

namespace fs = std::filesystem;

using namespace PluginSystem;

PluginHotloader::PluginHotloader(const std::string &path, PluginLoader &loaderRef)
: HotReloadSystem(path), loader(loaderRef)
{
}

static bool isSharedObject(const std::string &filename)
{
return filename.size() > 3 && filename.substr(filename.size() - 3) == ".so";
}

void PluginHotloader::onFileCreated(const std::string &filename)
{
if (!isSharedObject(filename))
return;

std::string fullpath = _directory + "/" + filename;

std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(150));

std::cout << "[PluginHotloader] New plugin detected: " << filename << "\n";
loader.loadPlugin(fullpath);
}

void PluginHotloader::onFileModified(const std::string &filename)
{
if (!isSharedObject(filename))
return;

std::string fullpath = _directory + "/" + filename;

std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(150));

std::cout << "[PluginHotloader] Plugin modified, reloading: " << filename << "\n";
loader.unloadPlugin(filename);
loader.loadPlugin(fullpath);
}

void PluginHotloader::onFileDeleted(const std::string &filename)
{
if (!isSharedObject(filename))
return;

std::cout << "[PluginHotloader] Plugin removed: " << filename << "\n";
loader.unloadPlugin(filename);
}

#include "PluginLoader.hpp"
#include <algorithm>

using namespace PluginSystem;

PluginLoader::PluginLoader(std::vector<ATool*>& tools, std::vector<AProtocol*>& protocols)
: toolList(tools), protocolList(protocols) {
std::cout << "[PluginLoader] Initialized." << std::endl;
}

PluginLoader::~PluginLoader() {
unloadAllPlugins();
std::cout << "[PluginLoader] All plugins unloaded." << std::endl;
}

void PluginLoader::loadAllPlugins(const std::string& pluginsDirectory) {
if (!fs::exists(pluginsDirectory) || !fs::is_directory(pluginsDirectory)) {
std::cerr << "[Loader] Directory not found: " << pluginsDirectory << std::endl;
return;
}

for (auto& entry : fs::recursive_directory_iterator(pluginsDirectory)) {
if (entry.is_regular_file() && entry.path().extension() == ".so")
loadPlugin(entry.path().string());
}

std::cout << "[Loader] Loaded " << toolList.size() << " tool(s) and "
<< protocolList.size() << " protocol(s)." << std::endl;
}

bool PluginLoader::loadPlugin(const std::string& path) {
std::string filename = fs::path(path).filename().string();

void* handle = dlopen(path.c_str(), RTLD_LAZY);
if (!handle) {
std::cerr << "[Loader] Failed to open plugin (" << path << "): " << dlerror() << std::endl;
return false;
}

dlerror();
using CreateInstanceFn = IPlugin* (*)();
CreateInstanceFn instance = reinterpret_cast<CreateInstanceFn>(dlsym(handle, "instance"));
const char* error = dlerror();
if (error) {
std::cerr << "[Loader] Missing factory symbol in " << path << ": " << error << std::endl;
dlclose(handle);
return false;
}

IPlugin* plugin = instance();
if (!plugin) {
std::cerr << "[Loader] Failed to create plugin instance: " << path << std::endl;
dlclose(handle);
return false;
}

if (plugin->getType() == PluginType::TOOL) {
auto* tool = dynamic_cast<ATool*>(plugin);
if (tool) {
toolList.push_back(tool);
std::cout << "[Loader] Loaded Tool: " << filename << std::endl;
}
} else if (plugin->getType() == PluginType::PROTOCOL) {
auto* proto = dynamic_cast<AProtocol*>(plugin);
if (proto) {
protocolList.push_back(proto);
std::cout << "[Loader] Loaded Protocol: " << filename << std::endl;
}
}

pluginHandles[filename] = handle;
pluginInstances[filename] = plugin;
return true;
}

bool PluginLoader::unloadPlugin(const std::string& filename) {
auto itHandle = pluginHandles.find(filename);
auto itInstance = pluginInstances.find(filename);
if (itHandle == pluginHandles.end() || itInstance == pluginInstances.end())
return false;

IPlugin* plugin = itInstance->second;
auto type = plugin->getType();

if (type == PluginType::TOOL)
toolList.erase(std::remove(toolList.begin(), toolList.end(), plugin), toolList.end());
else if (type == PluginType::PROTOCOL)
protocolList.erase(std::remove(protocolList.begin(), protocolList.end(), plugin), protocolList.end());

delete plugin;
dlclose(itHandle->second);

pluginHandles.erase(itHandle);
pluginInstances.erase(itInstance);

std::cout << "[Loader] Unloaded: " << filename << std::endl;
return true;
}

void PluginLoader::unloadAllPlugins() {
std::vector<std::string> filenames;
filenames.reserve(pluginInstances.size());

for (const auto& [filename, _] : pluginInstances)
filenames.push_back(filename);

for (const auto& filename : filenames)
unloadPlugin(filename);

pluginHandles.clear();
pluginInstances.clear();
}

#include "ToolScheduler.hpp"

#include <iostream>

namespace PluginSystem {

ToolScheduler::ToolScheduler(std::vector<ATool *> &tools)
: _tools(tools)
{}

ToolScheduler::~ToolScheduler()
{
stop();
}

void ToolScheduler::start()
{
if (_running.exchange(true))
return;
_thread = std::thread(&ToolScheduler::scheduleLoop, this);
}

void ToolScheduler::stop()
{
if (!_running.exchange(false))
return;
if (_thread.joinable())
_thread.join();
}

void ToolScheduler::scheduleLoop()
{
using Clock = std::chrono::steady_clock;
using Ms    = std::chrono::milliseconds;

std::unordered_map<ATool *, Clock::time_point> lastRun;

while (_running) {
const auto now = Clock::now();

for (ATool *tool : _tools) {
const uint64_t interval = tool->getIntervalMs();
if (interval == 0)
continue;

auto it = lastRun.find(tool);
if (it == lastRun.end()) {
lastRun[tool] = now;
continue;
}

const auto elapsed = std::chrono::duration_cast<Ms>(now - it->second);
if (static_cast<uint64_t>(elapsed.count()) >= interval) {
try {
tool->execute(nlohmann::json{});
} catch (const std::exception &e) {
std::cerr << "[ToolScheduler] execute() failed: " << e.what() << "\n";
}
it->second = now;
}
}

std::this_thread::sleep_for(Ms(10));
}
}

}

#include "CxxABI.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
std::string CxxABI::demangle(const char* name)
{
int   status = -4;
char* _out   = abi::__cxa_demangle(name, nullptr, nullptr, &status);
if (status != 0 && _out) free(_out);
if (status != 0) return std::string(name);
std::string result(_out);
free(_out);
return result;
}

std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos)
{
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> output;
std::string                         entry(symbolInfos);

std::size_t openP  = entry.find('(');
std::size_t closeP = entry.find(')');

if (openP == std::string::npos || closeP == std::string::npos)
return output;

std::string functionAndOffset = entry.substr(openP + 1, closeP);
std::size_t plusP             = functionAndOffset.find('+');

if (functionAndOffset.empty() || plusP == std::string::npos)
return output;

output.first =
CxxABI::demangle(functionAndOffset.substr(0, plusP).c_str());
std::stringstream ss;
ss << std::hex << functionAndOffset.substr(plusP + 1);
ss >> output.second;

return output;
}

// std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
// CxxABI::getBacktrace()
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> CxxABI::getBacktrace()
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> out;
int                                              TRACE_CHUNCK = 128;

int size = 0, out_size = 0;
do {
size += TRACE_CHUNCK;
void* trace[size + 1];
out_size = (int)backtrace(trace, size);
} while (out_size == size);
void* trace[out_size + 1];

backtrace(trace, out_size);
char** symbols = backtrace_symbols(trace, out_size);
if (symbols) {
for (int i = 0; i < out_size; ++i) {
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> symbol =
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(symbols[i]);
if (symbol.first.empty()) break;
out.push_back(symbol);
}
free(symbols);
}

return out;
}
}

namespace Flint
{
/**
     * Wrapper around usefull C++/C low level functions
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Got demangle, backtrace...
     *
     * @tip
     * This class is only made of static functions. It does not contain
     * constructor, nor destructor This means that you don't need to instanciate
     * it, and only use it as if it was a namespace.
     */
class CxxABI
{
public:
CxxABI() = delete;

/**
             * Demangle a C++ name.
             *
             * This is usefull for : Class names, backtrace...
             *
             * @return string containing : demangled name, or original name, if
             * demangle did not work
             */
static std::string demangle(const char* name);

/**
             * Get functions infos from symbol extraction
             *
             * @return A pair with the function names demangled, and the offset
             * from the function
             *
             * @warning
             * The function name is not EXACTLY the real name of the function.
             * Also, the offset DOES NOT correspond to the line number, but the
             * offset of asm instructions
             */
static std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos);

/**
             * Retreive the execution trace
             *
             * @return Vector of pair<pair<string, size_t>, string>. I'll
             * explain
             *
             * The idea is to retrieive the file, line, and function. So that's
             * what we do : pair or : (file, line), function
             *
             * @warning
             * To correctly grab function names, you will need to add
             * compilation flag "-rdynamic" See this lib's CMakeLists.txt for
             * implementation. This will export ALL symbols. If NOT done, you
             * will get offsets from binary. It's ok, but quite disgusting on
             * output.
             */
static std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>
getBacktrace();
};
}

#include "Exceptions.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
Exception::Exception(
const std::string&                                          what,
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> infos):
_infos(infos)
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> stackTrace =
CxxABI::getBacktrace();
std::stringstream ss;

ss << "[" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE
<< this->getClassNameFromStackTrace(stackTrace)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET;

if (!infos.first.first.empty() && !infos.second.empty()) {
ss << " in " << Flint::Colors::F_YELLOW << infos.second
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " << Flint::Colors::F_BLUE
<< infos.first.first.substr(
infos.first.first.find_last_of("/\\") + 1)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< infos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}
ss << "] " << what;

for (std::size_t i = 2; i < stackTrace.size(); i++) {
std::string funcName = stackTrace[i].first;
std::size_t openP    = funcName.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) funcName += "()";
funcName = funcName.substr(0, funcName.find("(") + 1) + "[...])";
ss << std::endl
<< " -> " << Flint::Colors::CYAN << funcName
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << "0x"
<< std::hex << stackTrace[i].second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}

this->_what = ss.str();
}

std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
Exception::getInfos() const
{
return this->_infos;
}

std::string Exception::getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>& stackTrace) const
{
if (stackTrace.size() <= 2) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string classNameSymbol = stackTrace[2].first.substr();
std::size_t openP           = classNameSymbol.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string functionName = classNameSymbol.substr(0, openP);
std::size_t lastColon    = functionName.rfind("::");
if (lastColon == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";
// Remove duplicate
return functionName.substr(0, lastColon);
}

std::string Exception::show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> fileInfos) const
{
std::stringstream ss;
ss << "Exception caught on " << Flint::Colors::F_GREEN
<< fileInfos.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << " at "
<< Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << fileInfos.first.first
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< fileInfos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl;

ss << this->_what;
return ss.str();
}

const char* Exception::what() const noexcept
{
return this->_what.c_str();
}

std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Exceptions::Exception& obj)
{
os << obj.what();
return os;
}

#ifndef INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP
#define INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP

#include "../Colors/Colors.hpp"
#include "../CxxABI/CxxABI.hpp"
#include "../Headers.hpp"
#include "../hpp/Preprocessor.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
/**
     * Wrapper arround default
     * [std::exception](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/error/exception)
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Store the the exception description as
     * [std::string](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/string/basic_string), and
     * store some usefull localisation informations: Filename, Line, and
     * Function Name. This is automated using the throw_exception preprocessor
     * directive.
     *
     * @tip
     * Take a look at the NotImplementedError to see how easy it is to implmeent
     * your own Flint::Exception.
     */
class Exception: public std::exception
{
protected:
std::string                                                 _what;
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> _infos;

public:
/**
             * Exception constructor. This need to be called, even on child
             * classes.
             *
             * @param what Define the exception's description
             * @param infos Might contain positional infos
             *
             * @warning
             * You should **NOT** use any excpetion constructor manually.
             * While they might work, the prefered way is to use the
             * throw_exception preprocessor directive. Using it will only
             * require you to register what type of excpetion you want to throw,
             * and what message you want to add, and will take care of putting
             * file name, line, and function name
             */
Exception(const std::string&     what  = "An exception occured !",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {
{"", 0},
""
});

/**
             * Retreive given infos (filename, line, and function name)
             *
             * @return The actual infos
             */
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
getInfos() const;

/**
             * Try to retreive the Exception class name from the StackTrace.
             *
             * @param stackTrace A stack trace, formated like
             * CxxABI::getBacktrace()
             *
             * @warning
             * This function **DOES NOT WORK 100% OF THE TIME**.
             * It is really dependant on the environement, and can return
             * undefined without aparent reason. It should **NOT** be used for
             * anything else than debug or display.
             *
             * @return The actual class name or "[Class name undefined]"
             */
std::string getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>&
stackTrace) const;

/**
             * Display the error, given specific positional infos (like the
             * constructor).
             *
             * @param infos Positional informations, like the constructor, used
             * to display the infos.
             *
             * @note
             * This function, while being usable without, should be used through
             * the catch_exception preprocessor directive. This preprocessor
             * directive let you display Python like excpetion trace.
             *
             * @return The formated exception, ready to be displayed.
             */
std::string show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos) const;

/**
             * Required override, deprecated by show and catch_exception.
             *
             * @info
             * This is the required std::exception override.
             * It'll return the _what as a const char*.
             * While this will work, prefer using the show function or the
             * catch_excpetion preprocessor directive :
             *
             * @example
             * ```cpp
             * try {
             *     throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::Exception, "Sample testing
             * exception"); } catch (const Flint::Exceptions::Exception& e) {
             *     std::cerr << catch_exception(e) << std::endl;
             * }
             * ```
             *
             * @return The _what as a C string
             */
const char* what() const noexcept override;

/**
             * operator<< override
             *
             * @abstract
             * This will simply append the _what to the ostream
             */
friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&                os,
const Exceptions::Exception& obj);
};

/**
     * Sample Flint::Exceptions::Exception to register NotImplemented features
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * @tip
     * You should use that Exception as a C++ Implementation of the Rust todo!()
     * macro.
     *
     * @example
     * ```cpp
     * throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::NotImplementedError, "This function
     * haven't been implemented as of today !");
     * ```
     */
class NotImplementedError: public Flint::Exceptions::Exception
{
public:
NotImplementedError(const std::string& what =
"Asked action haven't been implemented yet.",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {{"", 0}, ""}):
Exception(what, infos)
{
}
};
}

#endif

#include "Init.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
static void unhandeled_exception()
{
try {
std::rethrow_exception(std::current_exception());
} catch (const std::exception& e) {
const Flint::Exceptions::Exception* exception =
dynamic_cast<const Flint::Exceptions::Exception*>(&e);
if (exception != nullptr) {
std::cerr << Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " Uncaught Flint exception " << Flint::Colors::RED
<< "/!\\" << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl
<< *exception << std::endl;
} else {
std::cerr << Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " Uncaught exception "
<< Flint::Colors::RED << "/!\\"
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl
<< e.what() << std::endl;
}
}
}

void Init()
{
std::set_terminate(unhandeled_exception);
}
}

#include "Logging.hpp"

#include <optional>

namespace Flint
{
Logging& Logging::get()
{
static Logging instance;
return instance;
}

void Logging::set_log_on_stderr(bool log_on_stderr)
{
Logging::get().log_on_stderr = log_on_stderr;
}

void Logging::set_log_level(LogLevel level)
{
Logging::get().display_level = level;
}

void Logging::set_error_level(LogLevel level)
{
Logging::get().error_level = level;
}

void Logging::display_positional_infos(
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
positional_infos)
{
std::cout << "(at " << Flint::Colors::CYAN
<< positional_infos.first.first << Flint::Colors::RESET << ":"
<< Flint::Colors::PURPLE << positional_infos.first.second
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " in " << Flint::Colors::BLUE
<< positional_infos.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << ") ";
}

void Logging::debug(
const std::string& message,
std::optional<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
positional_infos)
{
if (Logging::get().display_level > Flint::LogLevel::DEBUG) return;

auto out = (Logging::get().log_on_stderr ? &std::cerr : &std::cout);

*out << "[ " << Flint::Colors::GREY << "DEBG" << Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " ] ";

if (positional_infos)
Logging::display_positional_infos(*positional_infos);

*out << message << std::endl;

auto backtrace = Flint::CxxABI::getBacktrace();

for (auto entry: backtrace)
*out << "    in " << Flint::Colors::PURPLE << entry.first
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::GREEN
<< entry.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl;
*out << "== End Debug Backtrace ==" << std::endl;

if (Logging::get().error_level <= Flint::LogLevel::DEBUG)
throw_exception(Flint::TooHighLevelLoggingError, message);
}

void Logging::info(
const std::string& message,
std::optional<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
positional_infos)
{
if (Logging::get().display_level > Flint::LogLevel::INFO) return;

auto out = (Logging::get().log_on_stderr ? &std::cerr : &std::cout);

*out << "[ " << Flint::Colors::GREEN << "INFO" << Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " ] ";

if (positional_infos)
Logging::display_positional_infos(*positional_infos);

*out << message << std::endl;

if (Logging::get().error_level <= Flint::LogLevel::INFO)
throw_exception(Flint::TooHighLevelLoggingError, message);
}

void Logging::warning(
const std::string& message,
std::optional<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
positional_infos)

if (Logging::get().display_level > Flint::LogLevel::WARNING) return;

auto out = (Logging::get().log_on_stderr ? &std::cerr : &std::cout);

*out << "[ " << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << "WARN" << Flint::Colors::RESET
<< " ] ";

if (positional_infos)

*out << message << std::endl;

if (Logging::get().error_level <= Flint::LogLevel::WARNING)

}

void Logging::error(

std::optional<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>

if (Logging::get().display_level > Flint::LogLevel::ERROR) return;

if (positional_infos)

*out << message << std::endl;

void Logging::critical(

#ifndef INCLUDED_INSPECTION_HPP
#define INCLUDED_INSPECTION_HPP

#include "../CxxABI/CxxABI.hpp"
#include "../Headers.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
/**
     * Base class to implement simplet self-reflexion in C++
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * @tparam T Child class itself
     *
     * @tip
     * When creating a child class, define as follow :
     * ```cpp
     * class ChildClass: public Inspection<ChildClass>
     * ```
     * This will extand the ChildClass with the self-reflexion class Inspection
     *
     * @warning
     * If the class you want to extand as a reflected class will be a parent
     * class (I.E. You will create childs of this "target" class), you might
     * want to mark inheritence as **virtual**. This is needed if your child
     * class _also_ need to be reflected. This way, there is no confilict
     * between the parent reflexion, and the child reflexion.
     */
template<typename T>
class Inspection
{
public:
/**
             * Return the name of the reflected class
             *
             * @return The demangled name of the class, or just the name, if
             * demangleing failed
             */
virtual std::string getClassName() const final
{
return CxxABI::demangle(typeid(T).name());
}

// virtual std::unordered_set<std::pair<std::string, std::string>>
// getAttributes();

/**
             * Return a string representation of the class
             *
             * @tip
             * This function is not intended to be used.
             * This is because this functions display the reflection.
             * You might prefer to override this function in yout child
             * class to display informations that are more relevant to
             * your specific class.
             *
             * @example
             * You have a Macro available to make the str function really
             * easilly :
             * ```cpp
             * display_attr(attribute)
             * ```
             * Which will automaticly translate to
             * ```cpp
             * Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << "attribute" << Flint::Colors::RESET <<
             * "=" << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute <<
             * Flint::Colors::RESET
             * ```
             * There is also a `make_str` macro, which you can use as follow :
             * ```cpp
             * return make_str("a=12" << ", " << "b=5");
             * ```
             * that becomes
             * ```cpp
             * return ({std::stringstream ss; ss << "<" << this->getClassName()
             * << " at " << std::hex << this << ": " << "a=12" << ", " << "b=5"
             * << ">"; ss.str()})
             * ```
             * It means you can define the str function of your class as like
             * that :
             * ```cpp
             * // Provided you have 3 attributes called a, b and c
             * return make_str(display_attr(a) << ", " << display_attr(b) << ",
             * " << display_attr(c));
             * ```
             *
             * @return The string representation of the class
             */
virtual std::string str() const
{
std::stringstream ss;

ss << "<" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE << this->getClassName()
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " << Flint::Colors::YELLOW
<< std::hex << this << Flint::Colors::RESET << ">";

return ss.str();
}

/** Overload the operator << to use the str method of the object
             *
             * @tparam T Reflected class
             *
             * @param os Reference of ostream (I.E. std::cout, std::cerr...)
             *
             * @param obj Object reference to be displayed
             *
             * @return Param os
             */
friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&        os,
const Inspection<T>& obj)
{
os << obj.str();
return os;
}
};
}


#endif

#ifndef INCLUDED_PREPROCESSOR_HPP
#define INCLUDED_PREPROCESSOR_HPP

#if defined(__GNUC__)
#define __FUNCTION__ __PRETTY_FUNCTION__
#elif defined(_MSC_VER)
#define __FUNCTION__ __FUNCSIG__
#else
#define __FUNCTION__ __func__
#endif

#define __POSITION_INFOS__                                                    \
    std::pair                                                                 \
    {                                                                         \
        std::pair{std::string(__FILE__), __LINE__}, std::string(__FUNCTION__) \
    }

#define throw_exception(exception, message)       \
    throw(exception(message, __POSITION_INFOS__))
#define catch_exception(exception) exception.show(__POSITION_INFOS__)

// Inspection
#define display_attr(attribute)                                        \
    Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << #attribute << Flint::Colors::RESET << "=" \
                          << Flint::Colors::RED << this->attribute     \
                          << Flint::Colors::RESET
#define make_str(display)                                            \
    ({                                                               \
        std::stringstream ss;                                        \
        ss << Flint::Colors::RESET << "<" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE \
           << this->getClassName() << Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " \
           << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << std::hex << this << std::dec  \
           << Flint::Colors::RESET << ": " << display << ">";        \
        ss.str();                                                    \
    })

#define log_debug(message)                                                 \
    Flint::Logging::debug(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_info(message)  Flint::Logging::info(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_warn(message)  Flint::Logging::warning(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_error(message) Flint::Logging::error(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))
#define log_critical(message)                             \
    Flint::Logging::critical(message, std::make_optional(__POSITION_INFOS__))

#endif

#include "DockerHandler.hpp"

namespace DockerAPI
{
DockerHandler::DockerHandler()
{
this->_curl = curl_easy_init();
if (!this->_curl) {
throw_exception(DockerAPI::CurlError,
"Error running curl_easy_init: NULL output");
}

curl_easy_setopt(this->_curl,
CURLOPT_UNIX_SOCKET_PATH,
DOCKER_SOCKET_URL);
curl_easy_setopt(this->_curl,
CURLOPT_WRITEFUNCTION,
DockerHandler::callback_function);

curl_easy_setopt(this->_curl, CURLOPT_WRITEDATA, this);
}

DockerHandler::~DockerHandler()
{
curl_easy_cleanup(this->_curl);
}

DockerHandler &DockerHandler::get()
{
static DockerHandler instance;
return instance;
}

size_t DockerHandler::callback_function(void  *buffer,
size_t sz,
size_t n,
void  *f)
{
DockerHandler *_this  = static_cast<DockerHandler *>(f);
_this->_buffer       += std::string((char *)buffer, sz * n);

return sz * n;
}

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string>
DockerHandler::dockerRawGet(const std::string &url)
{
DockerHandler &_this = DockerHandler::get();
std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_this._curlMutex);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POST, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPGET, 1L);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_URL, url.c_str());
CURLcode curl_output = curl_easy_perform(_this._curl);

long http_status;

curl_easy_getinfo(_this._curl, CURLINFO_RESPONSE_CODE, &http_status);

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string> output(curl_output,
http_status,
_this._buffer);

_this._buffer.clear();

return output;
}

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string>
DockerHandler::dockerRawPost(const std::string &url,
const std::string &data)
{
DockerHandler &_this = DockerHandler::get();
std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_this._curlMutex);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPGET, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POST, 1L);

struct curl_slist *headers = NULL;
headers = curl_slist_append(headers, "Content-Type: application/json");
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, headers);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, data.c_str());
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE, data.size());

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_URL, url.c_str());

CURLcode curl_output = curl_easy_perform(_this._curl);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POST, 0L);

long http_status;

curl_easy_getinfo(_this._curl, CURLINFO_RESPONSE_CODE, &http_status);

curl_slist_free_all(headers);

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string> output(curl_output,
http_status,
_this._buffer);

_this._buffer.clear();

return output;
}

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string>
DockerHandler::dockerRawDelete(const std::string &url)
{
DockerHandler &_this = DockerHandler::get();
std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_this._curlMutex);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPGET, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POST, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDSIZE, 0L);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, "DELETE");

struct curl_slist *headers = NULL;
headers = curl_slist_append(headers, "Content-Type: application/json");
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, headers);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, "DELETE");

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_URL, url.c_str());

CURLcode curl_output = curl_easy_perform(_this._curl);

curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, nullptr);
curl_easy_setopt(_this._curl, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, nullptr);

long http_status;

curl_easy_getinfo(_this._curl, CURLINFO_RESPONSE_CODE, &http_status);

curl_slist_free_all(headers);

std::tuple<CURLcode, long, std::string> output(curl_output,
http_status,
_this._buffer);

_this._buffer.clear();

return output;
}
}

{
std::string normalizeProtocolName(std::string plugin_name)
{
const auto slash_pos = plugin_name.find_last_of("/\\");
if (slash_pos != std::string::npos)
plugin_name = plugin_name.substr(slash_pos + 1);

if (plugin_name.size() > 3 &&
plugin_name.substr(plugin_name.size() - 3) == ".so") {
plugin_name = plugin_name.substr(0, plugin_name.size() - 3);
}

std::replace(plugin_name.begin(), plugin_name.end(), '_', '-');

return plugin_name;
}

std::string underscoredProtocolName(std::string value)
{
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), '-', '_');
return value;
}

std::string dockerPortToNumeric(const std::string& docker_port)
{
const auto slash_pos = docker_port.find('/');
if (slash_pos == std::string::npos)
return docker_port;
return docker_port.substr(0, slash_pos);
}

std::string sanitizeSessionId(std::string value)
{
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), '/', '-');
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), '\\', '-');
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), ' ', '-');
return value;
}

std::string resolveSessionsSourcePath()
{
namespace fs = std::filesystem;

const char* configured = std::getenv("ASSET_BROKER_SESSIONS_DIR");
fs::path sessions_path;

auto normalizeForDockerHost = [](std::string value) {
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), '\\', '/');
return value;
};

auto isWindowsAbsolutePath = [](const std::string& value) {
return value.size() >= 3 &&
std::isalpha(static_cast<unsigned char>(value[0])) != 0 &&
value[1] == ':' &&
(value[2] == '/' || value[2] == '\\');
};

if (configured && *configured) {
std::string configured_value = configured;
if (isWindowsAbsolutePath(configured_value))
return normalizeForDockerHost(configured_value);

sessions_path = fs::path(configured_value);
if (sessions_path.is_absolute())
return normalizeForDockerHost(sessions_path.string());
} else {
const fs::path cwd = fs::current_path();
const fs::path cwd_candidate = cwd / "sessions";
const fs::path parent_candidate = cwd.parent_path() / "sessions";

if (fs::exists(cwd_candidate) || cwd.filename() == "asset-broker")
sessions_path = cwd_candidate;
else
sessions_path = parent_candidate;
}

std::error_code ec;
fs::create_directories(sessions_path, ec);

const auto absolute = fs::absolute(sessions_path, ec);
if (ec)
return normalizeForDockerHost(sessions_path.string());
return normalizeForDockerHost(absolute.string());
}

std::string resolvePluginsSourcePath()
{
namespace fs = std::filesystem;

const char* configured = std::getenv("ASSET_BROKER_PLUGINS_DIR");
fs::path plugins_path;

auto normalizeForDockerHost = [](std::string value) {
std::replace(value.begin(), value.end(), '\\', '/');
return value;
};

auto isWindowsAbsolutePath = [](const std::string& value) {
return value.size() >= 3 &&
std::isalpha(static_cast<unsigned char>(value[0])) != 0 &&
value[1] == ':' &&
(value[2] == '/' || value[2] == '\\');
};

if (configured && *configured) {
std::string configured_value = configured;
if (isWindowsAbsolutePath(configured_value))
return normalizeForDockerHost(configured_value);

plugins_path = fs::path(configured_value);
if (plugins_path.is_absolute())
return normalizeForDockerHost(plugins_path.string());
} else {
const fs::path cwd = fs::current_path();
const fs::path cwd_candidate = cwd / "enabled-plugins";
const fs::path parent_candidate = cwd.parent_path() / "enabled-plugins";

if (fs::exists(cwd_candidate) || cwd.filename() == "asset-broker")
plugins_path = cwd_candidate;
else
plugins_path = parent_candidate;
}

std::error_code ec;
fs::create_directories(plugins_path / "protocols", ec);

const auto absolute = fs::absolute(plugins_path, ec);
if (ec)
return normalizeForDockerHost(plugins_path.string());
return normalizeForDockerHost(absolute.string());
}

std::string resolvePluginsRuntimePath()
{
namespace fs = std::filesystem;

auto hasProtocolsDir = [](const fs::path& base_path) {
std::error_code ec;
return fs::exists(base_path / "protocols", ec) && !ec;
};

const char* runtime_dir = std::getenv("ASSET_BROKER_PLUGINS_RUNTIME_DIR");
if (runtime_dir && *runtime_dir) {
const fs::path configured(runtime_dir);
if (hasProtocolsDir(configured))
return configured.string();
}

const fs::path mounted_plugins("/plugins");
if (hasProtocolsDir(mounted_plugins))
return mounted_plugins.string();

const fs::path mounted_plugin("/plugin");
if (hasProtocolsDir(mounted_plugin))
return mounted_plugin.string();

return resolvePluginsSourcePath();
}

bool isSharedObjectPlugin(const std::filesystem::path& path)
{
auto extension = path.extension().string();
std::transform(extension.begin(), extension.end(), extension.begin(), [](unsigned char c) {
return static_cast<char>(std::tolower(c));
});
return extension == ".so" || extension == ".dll" || extension == ".dylib";
}

json scanProtocolPluginsDirectory(const std::filesystem::path& protocols_path)
{
namespace fs = std::filesystem;

std::error_code ec;
if (!fs::exists(protocols_path, ec) || ec)
return json::array();

json result = json::array();
for (const auto& entry: fs::directory_iterator(protocols_path, ec)) {
if (ec)
break;

if (!entry.is_regular_file() || !isSharedObjectPlugin(entry.path()))
continue;

const std::string protocol = normalizeProtocolName(entry.path().stem().string());
if (!protocol.empty())
result.push_back(protocol);
}

std::sort(result.begin(), result.end());
result.erase(std::unique(result.begin(), result.end()), result.end());
return result;
}

class ProtocolPluginsHotReloadWatcher: public HotReloadSystem
{
public:
ProtocolPluginsHotReloadWatcher(
const std::string& directory,
std::function<void()> on_change):
HotReloadSystem(directory),
_onChange(std::move(on_change))
{
}

protected:
void onFileCreated(const std::string& filename) override
{
if (isSharedObjectPlugin(std::filesystem::path(filename)))
_onChange();
}

void onFileModified(const std::string& filename) override
{
if (isSharedObjectPlugin(std::filesystem::path(filename)))
_onChange();
}

void onFileDeleted(const std::string& filename) override
{
if (isSharedObjectPlugin(std::filesystem::path(filename)))
_onChange();
}

private:
std::function<void()> _onChange;
};

class ProtocolPluginsRegistry
{
public:
static ProtocolPluginsRegistry& instance()
{
static ProtocolPluginsRegistry registry;
return registry;
}

json list()
{
ensureStarted();

std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_mutex);
return _plugins;
}

private:
ProtocolPluginsRegistry() = default;

~ProtocolPluginsRegistry()
{
if (_watcher)
_watcher->stop();
}

void ensureStarted()
{
std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_mutex);
if (_started)
return;

_protocolsPath = std::filesystem::path(resolvePluginsRuntimePath()) / "protocols";
_plugins = scanProtocolPluginsDirectory(_protocolsPath);

std::error_code ec;
if (std::filesystem::exists(_protocolsPath, ec) && !ec) {
_watcher = std::make_unique<ProtocolPluginsHotReloadWatcher>(
_protocolsPath.string(),
[this]() { this->refresh(); });
_watcher->start();
}

_started = true;
}

void refresh()
{
std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(_mutex);
_plugins = scanProtocolPluginsDirectory(_protocolsPath);
}

private:
std::mutex _mutex;
bool _started = false;
std::filesystem::path _protocolsPath;
json _plugins = json::array();
std::unique_ptr<ProtocolPluginsHotReloadWatcher> _watcher;
};

std::string resolveComposeProjectName()
{
const char* configured = std::getenv("COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME");
if (configured && *configured)
return configured;
return "asset-broker";
}
}

ListenerSystem::ListenerSystem() {}

ListenerSystem::~ListenerSystem() {}

ListenerSystem& ListenerSystem::get()
{
static ListenerSystem instance;
return instance;

// load all running listeners (containers starting with "listener_")
void ListenerSystem::loadListeners()
{
log_info("Loading listeners...");

ListenerSystem::get()._listenersIds.clear();

auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawGet(
"http://localhost/containers/json?all=true");

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK)
throw_exception(DockerAPI::CurlError,
std::string("Invalid output from curl: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));

if (std::get<1>(output) != 200)
throw_exception(DockerAPI::CurlError,
std::string("Invalid curl HTTP code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)));

json result = json::array();
json input  = json::parse(std::get<2>(output));

for (const auto& el: input) {
if (el["Names"].get<std::vector<std::string>>()[0].rfind(
"/listener_",
0) == 0) {
log_debug("Loaded listener with id: " +
el["Id"].get<std::string>());

ListenerSystem::get()._listenersIds.push_back(
el["Id"].get<std::string>());
}
}

log_debug("Loaded containers");
}

json ListenerSystem::listListeners()
{
auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawGet(
"http://localhost/containers/json?all=true");

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to list listeners: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(output) != 200) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to list listeners: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)));
}

json input  = json::parse(std::get<2>(output));
json result = json::array();

for (const auto& el: input) {
if (!el.contains("Names") ||
!el["Names"].is_array() ||
el["Names"].empty()) {
continue;
}

const auto name = el["Names"][0].get<std::string>();
if (name.rfind("/listener_", 0) != 0) {
continue;
}

json item;
item["id"]      = el.value("Id", "");
item["name"]    = name.size() > 1 ? name.substr(1) : name;
item["image"]   = el.value("Image", "");
item["state"]   = el.value("State", "");
item["status"]  = el.value("Status", "");
item["created"] = el.value("Created", 0);
item["ports"]   = el.contains("Ports") ? el["Ports"] : json::array();

result.push_back(item);
}

return result;
}

json ListenerSystem::listProtocolPlugins()
{
return ProtocolPluginsRegistry::instance().list();
}

json ListenerSystem::getListener(const std::string& id)
{
auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawGet(
"http://localhost/containers/" + id + "/json");

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to get listener: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(output) == 404) {
throw_exception(ListenerError, "Listener not found");
}

if (std::get<1>(output) / 100 != 2) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to get listener: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)) +
std::string("; Response: ") +
std::get<2>(output));
}

json input = json::parse(std::get<2>(output));
const auto name = input.value("Name", "");
if (name.rfind("/listener_", 0) != 0) {
throw_exception(ListenerError, "Listener not found");
}

json result;
result["id"]     = input.value("Id", "");
result["name"]   = name.size() > 1 ? name.substr(1) : name;
result["image"]  = input.contains("Config") ? input["Config"].value("Image", "") : "";
result["state"]  = input.contains("State") ? input["State"].value("Status", "") : "";
result["status"] = input.contains("State") ? input["State"].value("Status", "") : "";
result["ports"]  = input.contains("HostConfig") && input["HostConfig"].contains("PortBindings")
? input["HostConfig"]["PortBindings"]
: json::object();

return result;
}

/// creates a container with
std::string ListenerSystem::createListener(
const std::string&                         plugin_name,
const std::pair<std::string, std::string>& port_config,
const std::string&                         protocol_host,
const std::string&                         session_id)
{
log_info("Creating listener...");

log_debug(std::string("Arguments: plugin_name: \"") +
plugin_name +
"\"; port_config: {" +
port_config.first +
", " +
port_config.second +
"}");

std::string listener_name =
std::string("listener_") + plugin_name + "-" + port_config.second;
std::replace(listener_name.begin(), listener_name.end(), '/', '-');

const std::string protocol = normalizeProtocolName(plugin_name);
const std::string protocol_port = dockerPortToNumeric(port_config.first);
const std::string resolved_session_id =
session_id.empty() ? sanitizeSessionId(listener_name) : sanitizeSessionId(session_id);
const std::string sessions_source_path = resolveSessionsSourcePath();
const std::string plugins_source_path = resolvePluginsSourcePath();
const std::string compose_project_name = resolveComposeProjectName();

std::string protocol_bind_host = "0.0.0.0";
std::string protocol_target_host = protocol_host;
const std::string protocol_key = underscoredProtocolName(protocol);

if (protocol_key == "tcp_bind") {
if (!protocol_host.empty())
protocol_bind_host = protocol_host;
} else if (protocol_target_host.empty()) {
protocol_target_host = "host.docker.internal";
}

json j = {
{"Image",            "listener:latest"                            },
{"Labels",
{{"com.docker.compose.project", compose_project_name},
{"com.docker.compose.service", "dynamic-listener"},
{"asset-broker.listener.managed", "true"}}},
{"Env",
json::array({"LISTENER_NAME=" + listener_name,
"SESSION_ID=" + resolved_session_id,
"PROTOCOL=" + protocol,
"PROTOCOL_PORT=" + protocol_port,
"SESSIONS_ROOT=/sessions",
"PROTOCOLS_DIR=/plugin/protocols",
"PROTOCOL_BIND=" + protocol_bind_host,
"PROTOCOL_HOST=" + protocol_target_host})},

{"HostConfig",
{{"PortBindings",
{{port_config.first,
json::array({{{"HostPort", port_config.second}}})}}},
{"Mounts",
json::array({{{"Type", "bind"},
{"Source", sessions_source_path},
{"Target", "/sessions"}},
{{"Type", "bind"},
{"Source", plugins_source_path},
{"Target", "/plugin"}}})},
{"NetworkMode", "asset-broker-listeners"}}                      },

{"NetworkingConfig",
{{"EndpointsConfig",
{{"asset-broker-listeners",
{{"Aliases", json::array({listener_name})}}}}}}              }
};

auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawPost(
"http://localhost/containers/create?name=" + listener_name,
j.dump());

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to create listener container: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(output) / 100 != 2) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to create listener container: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)) +
std::string("; Response: ") +
std::get<2>(output));
}

json data = json::parse(std::get<2>(output));

auto start_output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawPost(
"http://localhost/containers/" +
data["Id"].get<std::string>() +
"/start",
"");

if (std::get<0>(start_output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to start listener container: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(start_output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(start_output) / 100 != 2) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to start listener container: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(start_output)) +
std::string("; Response: ") +
std::get<2>(start_output));
}

return data["Id"];
}

void ListenerSystem::deleteListener(const std::string& id)
{
log_info("Deleting listener...");

log_debug(std::string("Arguments: id: \"") + id + "\"");

auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawDelete(
"http://localhost/containers/" + id + "?force=true");

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to delete listener container: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(output) / 100 != 2) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string(
"Failed to delete listener container: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)));
}

log_info(std::string("Listener container deleted successfully: ") + id);
}

void ListenerSystem::deleteListeners()
{
log_info("Deleting all listener containers...");

auto output = DockerAPI::DockerHandler::dockerRawGet(
"http://localhost/containers/json?all=true");

if (std::get<0>(output) != CURLE_OK) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to list listeners for cleanup: Curl Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<0>(output)));
}

if (std::get<1>(output) / 100 != 2) {
throw_exception(
ListenerError,
std::string("Failed to list listeners for cleanup: Http Error Code: ") +
std::to_string(std::get<1>(output)) +
std::string("; Response: ") +
std::get<2>(output));
}

json input = json::parse(std::get<2>(output));
for (const auto &el: input) {
if (!el.contains("Names") || !el["Names"].is_array() || el["Names"].empty())
continue;

const auto name = el["Names"][0].get<std::string>();
if (name.rfind("/listener_", 0) != 0)
continue;

const auto id = el.value("Id", "");
if (id.empty())
continue;

try {
deleteListener(id);
} catch (const Flint::Exceptions::Exception &error) {
log_error(std::string("Failed to delete listener container ") + id + ": " + error.what());
} catch (const std::exception &error) {
log_error(std::string("Failed to delete listener container ") + id + ": " + error.what());
}
}

log_info("Listener container cleanup finished");
}

}

#include "CxxABI.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
std::string CxxABI::demangle(const char* name)
{
int   status = -4;
char* _out   = abi::__cxa_demangle(name, nullptr, nullptr, &status);
if (status != 0 && _out) free(_out);
if (status != 0) return std::string(name);
std::string result(_out);
free(_out);
return result;
}

std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos)
{
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> output;
std::string                         entry(symbolInfos);

std::size_t openP  = entry.find('(');
std::size_t closeP = entry.find(')');

if (openP == std::string::npos || closeP == std::string::npos)
return output;

std::string functionAndOffset = entry.substr(openP + 1, closeP);
std::size_t plusP             = functionAndOffset.find('+');

if (functionAndOffset.empty() || plusP == std::string::npos)
return output;

output.first =
CxxABI::demangle(functionAndOffset.substr(0, plusP).c_str());
std::stringstream ss;
ss << std::hex << functionAndOffset.substr(plusP + 1);
ss >> output.second;

return output;
}

// std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>>
// CxxABI::getBacktrace()
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> CxxABI::getBacktrace()
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> out;
int                                              TRACE_CHUNCK = 128;

int size = 0, out_size = 0;
do {
size += TRACE_CHUNCK;
void* trace[size + 1];
out_size = (int)backtrace(trace, size);
} while (out_size == size);
void* trace[out_size + 1];

backtrace(trace, out_size);
char** symbols = backtrace_symbols(trace, out_size);
if (symbols) {
for (int i = 0; i < out_size; ++i) {
std::pair<std::string, std::size_t> symbol =
CxxABI::getFuncInfos(symbols[i]);
if (symbol.first.empty()) break;
out.push_back(symbol);
}
free(symbols);
}

return out;
}
}

#ifndef INCLUDED_CXXABI_HPP
#define INCLUDED_CXXABI_HPP

#include "../Headers.hpp"

namespace Flint
{
/**
     * Wrapper around usefull C++/C low level functions
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Got demangle, backtrace...
     *
     * @tip
     * This class is only made of static functions. It does not contain
     * constructor, nor destructor This means that you don't need to instanciate
     * it, and only use it as if it was a namespace.
     */
class CxxABI
{
public:
CxxABI() = delete;

/**
             * Demangle a C++ name.
             *
             * This is usefull for : Class names, backtrace...
             *
             * @return string containing : demangled name, or original name, if
             * demangle did not work
             */
static std::string demangle(const char* name);

/**
             * Get functions infos from symbol extraction
             *
             * @return A pair with the function names demangled, and the offset
             * from the function
             *
             * @warning
             * The function name is not EXACTLY the real name of the function.
             * Also, the offset DOES NOT correspond to the line number, but the
             * offset of asm instructions
             */
static std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>
getFuncInfos(const char* symbolInfos);

/**
             * Retreive the execution trace
             *
             * @return Vector of pair<pair<string, size_t>, string>. I'll
             * explain
             *
             * The idea is to retrieive the file, line, and function. So that's
             * what we do : pair or : (file, line), function
             *
             * @warning
             * To correctly grab function names, you will need to add
             * compilation flag "-rdynamic" See this lib's CMakeLists.txt for
             * implementation. This will export ALL symbols. If NOT done, you
             * will get offsets from binary. It's ok, but quite disgusting on
             * output.
             */
static std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>
getBacktrace();
};
}

#endif

#include "Exceptions.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
Exception::Exception(
const std::string&                                          what,
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> infos):
_infos(infos)
{
std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>> stackTrace =
CxxABI::getBacktrace();
std::stringstream ss;

ss << "[" << Flint::Colors::F_PURPLE
<< this->getClassNameFromStackTrace(stackTrace)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET;

if (!infos.first.first.empty() && !infos.second.empty()) {
ss << " in " << Flint::Colors::F_YELLOW << infos.second
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << " at " << Flint::Colors::F_BLUE
<< infos.first.first.substr(
infos.first.first.find_last_of("/\\") + 1)
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< infos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}
ss << "] " << what;

for (std::size_t i = 2; i < stackTrace.size(); i++) {
std::string funcName = stackTrace[i].first;
std::size_t openP    = funcName.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) funcName += "()";
funcName = funcName.substr(0, funcName.find("(") + 1) + "[...])";
ss << std::endl
<< " -> " << Flint::Colors::CYAN << funcName
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::YELLOW << "0x"
<< std::hex << stackTrace[i].second << Flint::Colors::RESET;
}

this->_what = ss.str();
}

std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
Exception::getInfos() const
{
return this->_infos;
}

std::string Exception::getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>& stackTrace) const
{
if (stackTrace.size() <= 2) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string classNameSymbol = stackTrace[2].first.substr();
std::size_t openP           = classNameSymbol.find('(');
if (openP == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";

std::string functionName = classNameSymbol.substr(0, openP);
std::size_t lastColon    = functionName.rfind("::");
if (lastColon == std::string::npos) return "[Class name undefined]";
// Remove duplicate
return functionName.substr(0, lastColon);
}

std::string Exception::show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> fileInfos) const
{
std::stringstream ss;
ss << "Exception caught on " << Flint::Colors::F_GREEN
<< fileInfos.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << " at "
<< Flint::Colors::F_BLUE << fileInfos.first.first
<< Flint::Colors::RESET << ":" << Flint::Colors::F_RED
<< fileInfos.first.second << Flint::Colors::RESET << std::endl
<< std::endl;

ss << this->_what;
return ss.str();
}

const char* Exception::what() const noexcept
{
return this->_what.c_str();
}

std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& os, const Exceptions::Exception& obj)
{
os << obj.what();
return os;
}
}

docs.text:
#ifndef INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP
#define INCLUDED_EXCEPTIONS_HPP

#include "../Colors/Colors.hpp"
#include "../CxxABI/CxxABI.hpp"
#include "../Headers.hpp"
#include "../hpp/Preprocessor.hpp"

namespace Flint::Exceptions
{
/**
     * Wrapper arround default
     * [std::exception](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/error/exception)
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * Store the the exception description as
     * [std::string](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/string/basic_string), and
     * store some usefull localisation informations: Filename, Line, and
     * Function Name. This is automated using the throw_exception preprocessor
     * directive.
     *
     * @tip
     * Take a look at the NotImplementedError to see how easy it is to implmeent
     * your own Flint::Exception.
     */
class Exception: public std::exception
{
protected:
std::string                                                 _what;
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string> _infos;

public:
/**
             * Exception constructor. This need to be called, even on child
             * classes.
             *
             * @param what Define the exception's description
             * @param infos Might contain positional infos
             *
             * @warning
             * You should **NOT** use any excpetion constructor manually.
             * While they might work, the prefered way is to use the
             * throw_exception preprocessor directive. Using it will only
             * require you to register what type of excpetion you want to throw,
             * and what message you want to add, and will take care of putting
             * file name, line, and function name
             */
Exception(const std::string&     what  = "An exception occured !",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {
{"", 0},
""
});

/**
             * Retreive given infos (filename, line, and function name)
             *
             * @return The actual infos
             */
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>, std::string>
getInfos() const;

/**
             * Try to retreive the Exception class name from the StackTrace.
             *
             * @param stackTrace A stack trace, formated like
             * CxxABI::getBacktrace()
             *
             * @warning
             * This function **DOES NOT WORK 100% OF THE TIME**.
             * It is really dependant on the environement, and can return
             * undefined without aparent reason. It should **NOT** be used for
             * anything else than debug or display.
             *
             * @return The actual class name or "[Class name undefined]"
             */
std::string getClassNameFromStackTrace(
const std::vector<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>>&
stackTrace) const;

/**
             * Display the error, given specific positional infos (like the
             * constructor).
             *
             * @param infos Positional informations, like the constructor, used
             * to display the infos.
             *
             * @note
             * This function, while being usable without, should be used through
             * the catch_exception preprocessor directive. This preprocessor
             * directive let you display Python like excpetion trace.
             *
             * @return The formated exception, ready to be displayed.
             */
std::string show(std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos) const;

/**
             * Required override, deprecated by show and catch_exception.
             *
             * @info
             * This is the required std::exception override.
             * It'll return the _what as a const char*.
             * While this will work, prefer using the show function or the
             * catch_excpetion preprocessor directive :
             *
             * @example
             * ```cpp
             * try {
             *     throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::Exception, "Sample testing
             * exception"); } catch (const Flint::Exceptions::Exception& e) {
             *     std::cerr << catch_exception(e) << std::endl;
             * }
             * ```
             *
             * @return The _what as a C string
             */
const char* what() const noexcept override;

/**
             * operator<< override
             *
             * @abstract
             * This will simply append the _what to the ostream
             */
friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream&                os,
const Exceptions::Exception& obj);
};

/**
     * Sample Flint::Exceptions::Exception to register NotImplemented features
     *
     * @ingroup flint
     *
     * @tip
     * You should use that Exception as a C++ Implementation of the Rust todo!()
     * macro.
     *
     * @example
     * ```cpp
     * throw_exception(Flint::Exceptions::NotImplementedError, "This function
     * haven't been implemented as of today !");
     * ```
     */
class NotImplementedError: public Flint::Exceptions::Exception
{
public:
NotImplementedError(const std::string& what =
"Asked action haven't been implemented yet.",
std::pair<std::pair<std::string, std::size_t>,
std::string> infos = {{"", 0}, ""}):
Exception(what, infos)
{
}
};
}

#endif
